```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

#Load packages
```{r}
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(data.table) 
library(ggplot2)
library(purrr)
library(vegan)
library(MuMIn)


```



#Data cleaning chunk 1. Here and in the next several data cleaning chunks we are getting rid of duplicate observations, unknown behaviors, and dealing with undirected behaviors
```{r}
matrix.please<-function(x) {
  m<-as.matrix(x[,-1])
  rownames(m)<-x[,1]
  m
}


`2021_interactions_raw` <- read_csv(here("ch3 data","2021_interactions_raw.csv"))
raw.allbehavedata <- `2021_interactions_raw`



birdIDs_capture <- read_csv(here("ch3 data","2021_birdIDs.csv")) 
birdIDs <- birdIDs_capture %>%
  dplyr::select(mark_id3) %>%
  filter(mark_id3!="") # all 20 birds





bird.list <- sort(unique(birdIDs$mark_id3)) # change each time
length(bird.list)
bird.list
length(bird.list)
# list of all possible dyads
dyad.list <- expand.grid(bird.list, bird.list) #head(dyad.list)
names(dyad.list) <- c("actor", "subject")
dyad.list <- subset(dyad.list, actor!=subject)
dyad.list$dyadID <- paste(dyad.list$actor, dyad.list$subject, sep="-")
# list of days with observations
obsday.list <- as.character(unique(raw.allbehavedata$date)) #only days with observations
obsday.list.wday <- as.data.frame(obsday.list)
colnames(obsday.list.wday) <- "date"
obsday.list.wday$nday <- seq(1:length(obsday.list.wday$date))

```
#Data cleaning chunk 2; "other" and "unknown" behaviors
```{r}
behave<-raw.allbehavedata 
unique(behave$behavior)
# other
behave.other<-filter(behave, behavior=="other") %>% dplyr::select(-behavior) %>%
  rename(behavior=other) %>%
  dplyr::select(orderkey, sessionKEY, session_start_timeStamp, date, full_time, time, actor, subject, behavior, everything())
behave.exl.other<-filter(behave, behavior!="other") %>% 
  dplyr::select(-other)
behave.goodother<-bind_rows(behave.exl.other, behave.other)
# unknown
behave.unknown <- filter(behave.goodother, behavior=="unknown") 
behave.unknown$behavior[behave.unknown$behavior=="unknown"] <-"unk"
behave.unknown <- behave.unknown %>%
  unite(behavior, behavior, unknown, sep = "_")
behave$behavior[behave$behavior=="unk agonistic"] <-"unk_agonistic"
behave.exl.unk<-behave.goodother %>% filter(behavior!="unknown") %>%
  dplyr::select(-unknown)
behave.goodunk<-bind_rows(behave.unknown, behave.exl.unk)
behave<-behave.goodunk
head(behave)




```
#Data cleaning chunk 3; prepare cleaned df
```{r}
# keep only observations where the actor is in the list of bird IDs
behave.goodID <- subset(behave, actor %in% bird.list)
# keep only observations where the actor (found above) and the subject are both in the list of bird IDs
behave.goodID <- subset(behave.goodID, subject %in% bird.list)
#finds n behaviors by sessionKEY, date, time, actor, subject, behavior
behave.key <- behave.goodID %>% 
  dplyr::group_by(sessionKEY, date, full_time,actor, subject, behavior) %>% 
  tally() # counts behaviors that were observed within the same minute
#finds max n behaviors per actor by date, time, and behavior type. Return just that summary
behave.maxkey<- behave.key %>% 
  group_by(actor, subject, date, full_time, behavior) %>%
  slice(which.max(n)) %>% # n.aggXaggXkey if summarise, n if tally
  ungroup()
head(behave.maxkey)
#finds n disps per actor by date for summarized/trimmed data
behaveXday <- behave.maxkey %>% 
  group_by(actor, subject, date, full_time, behavior) %>% 
  summarise(n=sum(n))
head(behaveXday)
min(behaveXday$n)





unique(behaveXday$behavior)
# behaviors in long st_unstat (1 event at a time)
behaveXday.long <- behaveXday %>%
  uncount(n) %>%
  mutate(n=1)
sum(behaveXday.long$n)
sum(behaveXday$n)
head(behaveXday.long)
## DUPLICATE UNDIRECTED BEHAVIORS
# filter the undirected behaviors 
behaveXday.undirected <- behaveXday.long %>% 
  filter(grepl(pattern="each other",tolower(behavior)))
  
behaveXday.undirected1 <-behaveXday.long %>%
  filter( behavior=="s2s") # spar as well?
behaveXday.undirected <- bind_rows(behaveXday.undirected,behaveXday.undirected1 )
# duplicate undirected dataframe and change subject and actor
behaveXday.undirected.dupl <- behaveXday.undirected %>%
  rename(subject=actor, actor=subject) %>%
  dplyr::select(actor, subject, everything())
# bind both dataframes
behaveXday.directed_2021.comb <- bind_rows(behaveXday.undirected, behaveXday.undirected.dupl) 
behaveXday.undir2dir <- behaveXday.directed_2021.comb %>%
  separate( behavior,into = c("behavior", "direction"), sep = "_") %>%
  dplyr::select(-direction)
unique(behaveXday.undir2dir$behavior)
## COMBINE DATAFRAMES
# bind to directed dataframe
behaveXday.directed_2021 <- behaveXday.long %>% 
  filter(!grepl(pattern="each other",tolower(behavior)) & behavior!="s2s")
unique(behaveXday.directed_2021$behavior)
behaveXday.directed_2021 <- bind_rows(behaveXday.directed_2021, behaveXday.undir2dir)
behaveXday.directed_2021$behavior[behaveXday.directed_2021$behavior=="NN"] <- "nn"
behaveXday.directed_2021$behavior[behaveXday.directed_2021$behavior=="alloP"] <- "allop"
behaveXday.directed_2021$behavior[behaveXday.directed_2021$behavior=="allopreen"] <- "allop"
length(behaveXday.directed_2021$actor)
unique(behaveXday.directed_2021$behavior)
12454 + 2587 # 15041
head(behaveXday.directed_2021)



```
#Add obs session to df
```{r}
#add obs session
head(behaveXday.directed_2021)

behaveXday.directed_2021 <- ungroup(behaveXday.directed_2021)
behaveXday.directed_2021 <- arrange(behaveXday.directed_2021, date, full_time)
#procedure to add obs session

#store the first row as a separate df. It will be binded back on later 
row_1_obs <- behaveXday.directed_2021[1,]

#put events side by side. This will allow us to subtract consecutive times from one another to determine whether an event took place on a separate observation session. 
obs_period_pt_1 <- mutate(behaveXday.directed_2021, actor_2 = lead(actor),
                          subject_2 = lead(subject),                                   
                          full_time_2 = lead(full_time), 
                          date2 = lead(date),
                          behavior_2 = lead (behavior)
                          )




#add empty columns for observation period and date period
obs_period_pt_1$obs_period <- NA
obs_period_pt_1$date_period <- NA

q <- 1

#loop to calculate observation sessions. If consecutive events were on the same day but there was a long time between events, it is determined that the 2nd event took place in a separate observation session. The ticker goes up, and is reset if events took place on different days.changed time from 30 min to 35 minutes cuz one instance where 30 min just added an obs session by a bit
for (i in 1:nrow(obs_period_pt_1)) {ifelse(obs_period_pt_1$date[i] == obs_period_pt_1$date2[i]
                                           & obs_period_pt_1$full_time_2[i] - obs_period_pt_1$full_time[i] >= 1800, q <- q+1, q <- q)
  ifelse(obs_period_pt_1$date[i] != obs_period_pt_1$date2[i], q <- 1, q <- q)
  obs_period_pt_1$obs_period [i] <- q
}

#remove original columns and keep mutated columns. These will replace original columns
obs_period_pt_2 <- subset(obs_period_pt_1, select = c(date2,  full_time_2, actor_2,  subject_2, behavior_2, obs_period, date_period  ))

#get rid of the bottom, which will be full of NAs, as the last event in the original data does not have anything that comes after
obs_period_pt_3 <- slice(obs_period_pt_2, 1:(n() - 1))


#rename mutated columns with original names 
setnames(obs_period_pt_3, old = c('date2',  'full_time_2', 'actor_2',  'subject_2','behavior_2'), 
         new = c('date',  'full_time', 'actor',  'subject', 'behavior'))

#add observation period "1" onto first row that we subseted off, as the first event in the data has to take place in obs session 1
row_1_obs$obs_period <- 1 
row_1_obs$date_period <- NA

#ensure that row 1 matches the df
row_1_obs_ready <-  subset(row_1_obs, select = c(date,  full_time, actor,  subject, behavior, obs_period, date_period  ))

#bind first row and df together
obs_period_pt_4 <- rbind(row_1_obs_ready, obs_period_pt_3)
obs_period_pt_4$date_period <- paste(obs_period_pt_4$date,obs_period_pt_4$obs_period)


unique(obs_period_pt_4$obs_period)



head(obs_period_pt_4)
```

#day version bind bin number and stability period onto raw data 
```{r}
three_day_bins_2021 <- read_csv(here("ch3 data","2021_3daybins.csv"))
MOPA_power_score_2021 <- read_csv(here("ch3 data","MOPA_date_rank_2021.csv"))

head(three_day_bins_2021)
three_day_bins_2021$date <- as.Date(three_day_bins_2021$date, format = "%m/%d/%Y")

#filter for study period
three_day_bins_2021_sub <- three_day_bins_2021[(three_day_bins_2021$date >= "2021-05-10" & three_day_bins_2021$date <= "2021-07-04" ),]

#subset for only columns of interest and replace nas with appropriate bin number and type with appropriate type  (here its the character na)
three_day_bins_2021_sub <- subset(three_day_bins_2021_sub, select = c("date", "type", "rank_assessment_21")) %>%
  mutate(binpt2 = lead(rank_assessment_21), typept2 = lead(type)) %>%
  mutate(type = ifelse(rank_assessment_21 == "na", typept2, type ),
         rank_assessment_21 = ifelse(rank_assessment_21 == "na", binpt2, rank_assessment_21 )
         )
  

#remove integers from social stability type
three_day_bins_2021_sub$type <- gsub("[0-30]+", "", three_day_bins_2021_sub$type)

#subset for columns of interest
three_day_bins_2021_sub <- subset(three_day_bins_2021_sub, select = c("date", "type", "rank_assessment_21"))

three_day_bins_2021_sub$rank_assessment_21 <- as.numeric(three_day_bins_2021_sub$rank_assessment_21)

#add on column for stable and unstable periods
#figure out the phase for each bin/type combo.
three_day_bins_2021_sub <- three_day_bins_2021_sub %>%
  distinct(type, rank_assessment_21, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  #Sort the data by bin so that theyre processed in order to effectively  detect changes in phase 
  arrange(rank_assessment_21) %>%
  #detect previous rows type and return true when type changes. Turns that into a grouping variable so every time type changes the group number increases by 1
  mutate(type_change = type != lag(type, default = first(type)),
         type_group = cumsum(type_change)) %>%
  group_by(type_group) %>%
  #for each group of consecutive bins of the same type label, make the first row unstable and the following row stable
  mutate(phase = ifelse(row_number() == 1, "unstable", "stable")) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::select(rank_assessment_21, type, phase) %>%
  #bind back to original dataset
  right_join(three_day_bins_2021_sub, by = c("rank_assessment_21", "type"))

#convert bin to numeric for sorting
three_day_bins_2021_sub$rank_assessment_21 <- as.numeric(three_day_bins_2021_sub$rank_assessment_21)
three_day_bins_2021_sub <- arrange(three_day_bins_2021_sub,rank_assessment_21)

head(three_day_bins_2021_sub)
#subset for columns of interest
MOPA_power_score_2021_sub <- subset(MOPA_power_score_2021, select = c("date", "id", "rank"))


#filter main df so that df only contains crowds and displacements
aggresion_sub<- filter(obs_period_pt_4, behavior %in% c("crowd","displace", "chase"))



#filter for study period
aggresion_sub <- aggresion_sub[(aggresion_sub$date >= "2021-05-10" & aggresion_sub$date <= "2021-07-04" ),]


head(MOPA_power_score_2021)
MOPA_power_score_2021_sub <- subset(MOPA_power_score_2021, select = c("date", "id", "rank"))


#bind phase df and rank df onto main aggression df 
aggresion_sub_pt2 <- aggresion_sub %>% 
  left_join(three_day_bins_2021_sub, by = c("date")) %>%
  rename(bin=rank_assessment_21) %>%
  arrange(date, full_time) %>% 
  left_join(MOPA_power_score_2021_sub, by = c("date", "actor"= "id")) %>%
  rename(actor_rank = rank) %>%
  left_join(MOPA_power_score_2021_sub, by = c("date", "subject"= "id")) %>%
  rename(subject_rank = rank)


```


```{r}
#Prepare/order & give each row an id + numeric time
df <- aggresion_sub_pt2 %>%
  arrange(date_period, date, full_time) %>%
  mutate(.row = row_number(),
         tsec = as.numeric(full_time))  # seconds since midnight

#Previous-by-actor (used for  base A to B within 60s check) 
by_actor <- df %>%
  group_by(date_period, actor) %>%
  mutate(prev_subj_by_actor = lag(subject),
         prev_tsec_by_actor = lag(tsec)) %>%
  ungroup()

#Last involvement time for each participant (actor OR subject) 
# Build a long view with one entry per participant per event
long <- bind_rows(
  by_actor %>% transmute(date_period, .row, participant = actor, tsec),
  by_actor %>% transmute(date_period, .row, participant = subject, tsec)
) %>%
  arrange(date_period, tsec, .row) %>%
  group_by(date_period, participant) %>%
  mutate(prev_involve_tsec = lag(tsec)) %>%
  ungroup()

#For each original row, get the most recent involvement of either A or B before this row
last_involve <- long %>%
  group_by(date_period, .row) %>%
  summarise(last_involve_tsec = max(prev_involve_tsec, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(last_involve_tsec = ifelse(is.finite(last_involve_tsec), last_involve_tsec, NA_real_))

#Apply  rule of getting rid of consectuive 60 sec possible chases
aggression_no_repeat_custom <- by_actor %>%
  left_join(last_involve, by = c("date_period", ".row")) %>%
  mutate(
    #Base repeat condition. previous by same actor is the same subject and within 60s
    within_actor_repeat = !is.na(prev_tsec_by_actor) &
                          subject == prev_subj_by_actor &
                          (tsec - prev_tsec_by_actor) <= 60,

    #Detect if there was an intervening event involving A or B after that previous A to B
    interrupted_by_A_or_B = within_actor_repeat &
                            !is.na(last_involve_tsec) &
                            (last_involve_tsec > prev_tsec_by_actor),

    keep = !within_actor_repeat | interrupted_by_A_or_B
  ) %>%
  filter(keep) %>%
dplyr::select(-.row, -tsec,
         -prev_subj_by_actor, -prev_tsec_by_actor,
         -last_involve_tsec, -within_actor_repeat, -interrupted_by_A_or_B, -keep)


obs <- aggression_no_repeat_custom$date_period



dater <- unique(obs)
datex <- c(1: length(unique(obs)))
actorer <- unique(aggression_no_repeat_custom$actor)
actex <- c(1:length(actorer))




#sort by time and date
opp_filt_binded_2021 <- aggression_no_repeat_custom %>%
  arrange(aggression_no_repeat_custom$date, aggression_no_repeat_custom$full_time)

colSums(is.na(opp_filt_binded_2021))

#bottom rows of df are full of NA rows from where bird was removed or didnt aggressively interact. Checked for NAs throughout rest of df. clean through rest so removing final NA rows 
#opp_filt_binded_2021 <- na.omit(opp_filt_binded_2021)
#to here
 
opp_aggression_list_2021_again <- list()
#separate data into list of each obs session each day for each bird
for(i in datex) { 
      for (g in actex){
    output <- filter(opp_filt_binded_2021, opp_filt_binded_2021$date_period == dater[i] & (subject == actorer[g] | actor == actorer[g]))
    if (nrow(output) < 1) {output [ nrow(output) + 1 , ] <- NA
    } 
        output <- mutate(output, counter = actorer[g])
    opp_aggression_list_2021_again[[paste0("list", dater[i], actorer[g])]] <- output}

}
 
#ensure df arranged by date and time
opp_aggression_list_2021_again <- lapply(opp_aggression_list_2021_again, function(x) arrange(x, date, full_time))





#modified data frame to put consecutive events side by side

opp_side_by_side <- lapply(opp_aggression_list_2021_again, function(x) mutate(x, actor_2 = lead(actor),
                                                                          actor_2_rank = lead(actor_rank), subject_2_rank = lead(subject_rank), 
                                                                                        subject_2 = lead(subject), 
                                                                                        full_time_2 = lead(full_time), 
                                                                                        date2 = lead(date))) 


```

#Calculate time threshold
```{r}
dateact1 <-  c(1:length(opp_side_by_side))

#rename df for calculation of time to opp in seconds
opp_side_by_side_time_2021 <- opp_side_by_side


#pick out opp along rule while keeping all dataframes in a list (yay!!!)
for(i in dateact1 ) {
  opp_side_by_side_time_2021[[i]] <- mutate(opp_side_by_side_time_2021[[i]], 
                                           initiated = ifelse(subject == counter
                                           & subject_2 == counter
                                           & subject_2_rank < actor_2_rank
                                           & actor != actor_2
                                           & subject == subject_2, "yes","no"), 
                                         rule_followed = ifelse( subject_2_rank < actor_2_rank
                                          & subject == counter
                                          & subject_2 == subject
                                          & actor != actor_2
                                          & subject_2 == counter
                                          & full_time_2 - full_time > 0
                                          & date2 == date,"yes","no"),time_to_opp = full_time_2 - full_time)
  
}




 #combine dataframes into one huge dataframe
 opp_time_huge_2021 <- bind_rows(opp_side_by_side_time_2021, .id = "column_label")
 
 
 

#get df where rule was followed
opp_time_length_st_unst_2021 <- subset(opp_time_huge_2021, rule_followed != "no")
 
 #subset for columns of interest
 opp_time_length_st_unst_2021 <- subset(opp_time_length_st_unst_2021, select = c(date, full_time, actor, actor_rank, subject, subject_rank,
                                                                         actor_2, actor_2_rank, subject_2, subject_2_rank, full_time_2, date2, counter, 
                                                                         initiated, rule_followed, time_to_opp, date_period, bin, behavior, type, phase ))
 

opp_time_length_st_unst_2021$time_to_opp <- as.numeric(opp_time_length_st_unst_2021$time_to_opp)
 

ggplot(opp_time_length_st_unst_2021,    aes(x = bin, y = time_to_opp, group = bin, colour = phase)) + geom_boxplot() +
     coord_cartesian( ylim = c(0,75))


ggplot(opp_time_length_st_unst_2021,    aes(x = bin, y = time_to_opp, group = bin, colour = type)) + geom_boxplot() +
     coord_cartesian( ylim = c(0,75))







```



#calculate number of rule follows
```{r}
#read in previously calculated thresholds (group 1 and 2)
summary.opp_length_all <- read_csv(here("ch3 data","summary.full_opp_length_st_unst_all.csv"))

#round threshold to whole number  
summary.opp_length_all$q95 <- round(summary.opp_length_all$q95)

# Merge both in and out variables onto the full actor-subject pair list

opp_filt_binded_2021_thresh <- opp_filt_binded_2021 %>%
  left_join(summary.opp_length_all, by = c("bin")) 


#opp_filt_binded_2021_thresh <- subset(opp_filt_binded_2021_thresh, select = -c(...1))




opp_aggression_list_thresh_2021 <- list()
dater <- unique(obs)
datex <- c(1: length(unique(obs)))
actorer <- unique(opp_filt_binded_2021_thresh$actor)
actex <- c(1:length(actorer))



#separate data into list of each obs session each day for each bird
for(i in datex) { 
        for (g in actex){
    output <- filter(opp_filt_binded_2021_thresh, opp_filt_binded_2021_thresh$date_period == dater[i] & (subject == actorer[g] | actor == actorer[g]))
    if (nrow(output) < 1) {output [ nrow(output) + 1 , ] <- NA
    } 
            output <- mutate(output, counter = actorer[g])
    opp_aggression_list_thresh_2021[[paste0("list", dater[i], actorer[g])]] <- output}

}



#modified data frame to put consecutive events side by side

opp_side_by_side_thresh <- lapply(opp_aggression_list_thresh_2021, function(x) mutate(x, actor_2 = lead(actor),
                                                                          actor_2_rank = lead(actor_rank), subject_2_rank = lead(subject_rank), 
                                                                                        subject_2 = lead(subject), 
                                                                                        full_time_2 = lead(full_time), 
                                                                                        date2 = lead(date))) 




opp_side_by_side_for_perm_2021 <- opp_side_by_side_thresh





dateact1 <-  c(1:length(opp_side_by_side_thresh))


#pick out opp along rule while keeping all dataframes in a list (yay!!!)
for(i in dateact1 ) {
  opp_side_by_side_thresh[[i]] <- mutate(opp_side_by_side_thresh[[i]], 
                                           initiated = ifelse(subject == counter
                                           & subject_2 == counter
                                           & subject_2_rank < actor_2_rank
                                           & actor != actor_2
                                           & subject == subject_2, "yes","no"), 
                                         rule_followed = ifelse( subject_2_rank < actor_2_rank
                                          & subject == counter
                                          & full_time_2 - full_time <= q95 
                                          & subject_2 == subject
                                          & actor != actor_2
                                          & subject_2 == counter
                                          & full_time_2 - full_time > 0
                                          & date2 == date,"yes","no"),time_to_opp = full_time_2 - full_time)
} 




#combine dataframes into one huge dataframe
opp_huge <- bind_rows(opp_side_by_side_thresh, .id = "column_label")

#sort by time and date
opp_huge_sorted <- opp_huge %>%
  arrange(opp_huge$date, opp_huge$full_time)


#need to check but i think it works to get rid of all "initiated no" rows to get rid of duplicates, as opp along 
#is literally possible whenever an aggressive event takes place

opp_huge_sorted_no_dupr <- subset(opp_huge_sorted, initiated != "no") 

opp_no_dupr_2021 <- subset(opp_huge_sorted_no_dupr, select = c(date, full_time, actor, actor_rank, subject, subject_rank, bin, behavior, type, phase, actor_2, actor_2_rank, subject_2, subject_2_rank,  full_time_2, date2, counter, initiated, rule_followed,time_to_opp))

table(opp_no_dupr_2021$rule_followed)

opp_real_2021 <- length(which(opp_no_dupr_2021$rule_followed == "yes"))





opp_doner_all_yes_2021 <-filter(opp_no_dupr_2021, rule_followed == "yes")



```


# individual rule following actor
```{r}

#test for individual use
unique(opp_doner_all_yes_2021$actor_2)

summary.opp.individ_2021 <- opp_doner_all_yes_2021 %>% group_by(date, phase, actor_2) %>%
  summarize (
    count =n())

summary.opp.individ_2021




# Plot
individ_opp_plot_2021 <- ggplot(summary.opp.individ_2021, aes(x = actor_2, y = count, colour = phase  )) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.6) + 
  facet_wrap(~ date) +
  theme_minimal()  +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust=0.5),
    axis.title.x = element_text( face = "bold",  margin = margin(t = 15)),  
    axis.title.y = element_text( face = "bold",  margin = margin(r = 15)),
    ) +
  labs(
    x = "Parakeet ID",  
    y = "Count of rule follows"   
  )

individ_opp_plot_2021




summary.opp.individ_2021 <- opp_doner_all_yes_2021 %>% group_by(date, bin, phase, type, actor_2) %>%
  summarize (
    count =n())

summary.opp.individ_2021



# Plot
individ_opp_plot_2021 <- ggplot(summary.opp.individ_2021, aes(x = actor_2, y = count, colour = type  )) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.6) + 
  facet_wrap(~ date) +
  theme_minimal()  +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust=0.5),
    axis.title.x = element_text( face = "bold",  margin = margin(t = 15)),  
    axis.title.y = element_text( face = "bold",  margin = margin(r = 15)),
    ) +
  labs(
    x = "Parakeet ID",  
    y = "Count of rule follows"   
  )

individ_opp_plot_2021

head(summary.opp.individ_2021)






#metrics per date 
metrics_opp_2021_actor <- summary.opp.individ_2021 %>%
  group_by(date) %>%
  summarise(
    Richness  = sum(count > 0, na.rm = TRUE),                    
    Diversity = if (sum(count, na.rm = TRUE) == 0) 0 else vegan::diversity(count, index = "shannon"),
    Evenness  = dplyr::if_else(Richness > 1, Diversity / log(Richness), NA_real_),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

summary.opp.individ_2021_no_act_count <- subset(summary.opp.individ_2021, select = -c(actor_2, count))
#keep only distinct rows 
summary.opp.individ_2021_no_act_count <- distinct(summary.opp.individ_2021_no_act_count)


#bind bin phase and type onto new metrics summary df
metrics_opp_2021_actor <- metrics_opp_2021_actor  %>% 
  left_join(summary.opp.individ_2021_no_act_count, by = "date")





#group size denominator = unique individuals in each group across both roles so proportional richness is comparable across groups)
#determine how many birds were in the experiment and did anything at each date
head(obs_period_pt_4)
df_4_group_size <- obs_period_pt_4[(obs_period_pt_4$date >= "2021-05-10" & obs_period_pt_4$date <= "2021-07-04" ),]



group_sizes <- df_4_group_size %>%
  group_by(date) %>%
  summarise(N = n_distinct(c(actor, subject), na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")


#add in rule type 
metrics_opp_2021_actor$rule <- "Opportunism"

#calculate proportional richness 
metrics_opp_2021_actor <- metrics_opp_2021_actor %>%
  left_join(group_sizes, by = "date") %>%
  mutate(
    Proportional_Richness = if_else(
      rule %in% c("Retaliation","Pile-on"),
      Richness / N,
      Richness / pmax(N - 1, 1)  # avoid divide-by-zero
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(date, rule)

metrics_opp_2021_actor

#write.csv(metrics_opp_2021_actor,  here("ch3 data","metrics_opp_2021_actor.csv"), row.names = FALSE)






ggplot(data = metrics_opp_2021_actor, aes(x = date, y = Diversity)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = 1)) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = type, size = 1)) +
 coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 3)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Diversity Across dates",
       x = "date", y = "Value") 




ggplot(data = metrics_opp_2021_actor, aes(x = date, y = Evenness)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = 1)) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = type, size = 1)) +
 coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Evenness Across dates",
       x = "date", y = "Value") 




ggplot(data = metrics_opp_2021_actor, aes(x = date, y = Proportional_Richness)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = 1)) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = type, size = 1)) +
 coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Proportional_Richness Across dates",
       x = "date", y = "Value") 

```

#randomize order of events
```{r}

#REMEMBER TO BIND THRESHOLD TO DF
start_time <- format(Sys.time(), "%H:%M:%S")



opp_obs_bin_2021 <- unique(opp_filt_binded_2021_thresh$bin)
opp_count_length_list_masterframe_2021 <- list()

pbouter = txtProgressBar(min=0, max = length(opp_obs_bin_2021), style = 3)

set.seed(249)

for(k in opp_obs_bin_2021) { 
  runouter <- k
  setTxtProgressBar(pbouter, k) #update progress bar
  loop.run_outer <- paste("outer run", runouter) 
  loop.run_outer
  
  opp_filt_binded_2021_rand <- subset(opp_filt_binded_2021_thresh, bin == k )  
  
  
  #remove extra columns that create spam in loop
  opp_filt_binded_2021_rand <- subset(opp_filt_binded_2021_rand, select = c(date, full_time, actor, subject, bin, behavior, obs_period, date_period, type, phase, actor_rank, subject_rank, counter, q95 ))
  
  
  date_x <- opp_filt_binded_2021_rand$date
  obs <- opp_filt_binded_2021_rand$date_period
  #separate data into a list with each df=a date so we can randomize within days
  #sort_by_date <- function(x, i, y) {y <- filter(x, date == i )
  #mutate(y, date_counter = i)}
  
  datelist <- list()
  dater <- unique(obs)
  datex <- c(1: length(unique(obs)))
  #boop <- filter(both_numbered_sorted, both_numbered_sorted$date == datex [2])
  
  #loop to create a separate dataframe for each bird that only contains interactions #pertaining to that bird.
  #Each dataframe is stored within a list to make the dataframes easy to work with
  #separate data into list of each obs session each day 
  for(i in datex) { 
    output <- filter(opp_filt_binded_2021_rand, opp_filt_binded_2021_rand$date_period == dater[i] )
    if (nrow(output) < 1) {output [ nrow(output) + 1 , ] <- NA
    } 
    datelist[[paste0("list", dater[i])]] <- output}
  
  
  
  
  #run ids should be 1 through however many times you want to run through the permutation
  runIDs <- seq(1,1000)
  
  #create an empty masterframe to be filled with each run of the permutation
  masterframe_opp_2021.st_unst.df <- data.frame(runIDs = character(),
                                                 actor = character(),
                                                 actor_rank = numeric(),
                                                 subject = character(),
                                                 subject_rank = numeric(),
                                                 date = character(),
                                                 full_time = integer(),
                                                 bin = integer(),
                                                 behavior = character(),
                                                 type = character(),
                                                 phase = character(),
                                                 actor_2 = character(),
                                                 actor_2_rank = numeric(),
                                                 subject_2 = character(),
                                                 subject_2_rank = numeric(),
                                                 full_time_2 = integer(),
                                                 date2 = character(),
                                                 q95 = numeric(),
                                                 counter = character(),
                                                 initiated = character(),
                                                 rule_followed = character(),
                                                 stringsAsFactors=FALSE)
  nruns <- 1000
  #pb = txtProgressBar(min=0, max = nruns, style = 3)
  
  #forloop starts here'
  for (r in 1:length(runIDs)) { #r = 1 is a test you can uncomment to see if it works before #running it 100 times
    #r = 1
    run <- r
    #setTxtProgressBar(pb, r) #update progress bar
    loop.run <- paste("inner run", run) 
    loop.run
    #for example, if r = 1, looprun will say run 1 which will allow you to keep each run's #data straight
    
    
    #loop.seed <- set.seed[r] didnt work so ignore. not needed
    
    
    #renamed data
    loop.datalist <- datelist
    xorder <- c()
    listlength <- as.numeric(length(loop.datalist))
    datex2 <- 1:listlength
    full_time_list <-c()
    loop.data.randomized.time.added <- c()
    #get rid of anything thats not actor and receiver (we will bind back on the date and time #later anyways to preserve
    #their order)
    
    #remove date from each df in the list
    #create a randomized sequence of numbers to bind to the subset data (ex. 2,6,1,3,5,4)
    #sort the data frame to put the randomized numbers in order from 1 to whatever and voila, #the order of events has been randomized (ex. from above 1,2,3,4,5,6)
    
    for(i in datex2) {
      full_time_list[[i]] <- subset(loop.datalist[[i]], select = c(full_time))
      loop.datalist[[i]] <- subset(loop.datalist[[i]], select = -c(full_time))
      
      xorder[[i]] <- data.frame(xorder = sample(1:length(loop.datalist[[i]]$actor),
                                                length(loop.datalist[[i]]$actor)))
      loop.datalist[[i]] <- mutate(loop.datalist[[i]], 
                                   xorderx = xorder[[i]]$xorder)
      loop.datalist[[i]] <-
        loop.datalist[[i]][order(loop.datalist[[i]]$xorderx),]
      #bind time back on
      loop.data.randomized.time.added[[i]] <- bind_cols(loop.datalist[[i]], full_time_list[[i]])
      loop.data.randomized.time.added[[i]] <- subset(loop.data.randomized.time.added[[i]], select = -c(xorderx))
      
    }
    
    practdrlist <- loop.data.randomized.time.added
    
    

#separate data into list of each obs session each day for each bird

actex <- c(1:length(actorer))
dater <- unique(obs)
practdrlist_split_by_bird <- c()

for(i in datex2) {
for (g in actex) {output <- subset(practdrlist[[i]], actor == actorer[g] |  subject == actorer[g])
#if one of the dfs in the list is empty, add NA
   if (nrow(output) < 1) {output [ nrow(output) + 1 , ] <- NA
    } 
  output <- mutate(output, counter = actorer[g])
  practdrlist_split_by_bird[[paste0("list", dater[i], actorer[g])]] <- output
}}
    
    
    opp_2021_rand_split_by_bird_ordered <- lapply(practdrlist_split_by_bird, function(x) arrange(x, date, full_time))
    
    
    
    
    #modified each data frame to put consecutive events side by side
    
    opp_rand_test_2021 <- lapply(opp_2021_rand_split_by_bird_ordered, function(x) mutate(x, actor_2 = lead(actor), 
                                                                                   actor_2_rank = lead(actor_rank), 
                                                                                   subject_2 = lead(subject), 
                                                                                   subject_2_rank = lead(subject_rank), 
                                                                                   full_time_2 = lead(full_time), 
                                                                                   date2 = lead(date))) 
    
    
    
    
    #successfully picked out opp rule while keeping all dataframes in a list (yay!!!)
    #loop to set conditionals to pick out opp
    
    #counter corresponds with which birds dataframe I am looking at and is used to control for false positives 
    #for example, if opp is detected for a bird whos specific dataframe I am not looking #at , this may be a false positive, as that birds interactions are not all listed in the #dataframe of another bird. In other words, the bird may have done something else to another #bird that is not listed, as it would be only listed in that specific birds dataframe
    dateact2 <-  c(1:length(opp_rand_test_2021))
    
    
    
    
    for(i in dateact2) {
      opp_rand_test_2021[[i]] <- mutate(opp_rand_test_2021[[i]], 
                                           initiated = ifelse(subject == counter
                                           & subject_2 == counter
                                           & subject_2_rank < actor_2_rank
                                           & actor != actor_2
                                           & subject == subject_2, "yes","no"), 
                                         rule_followed = ifelse( subject_2_rank < actor_2_rank
                                          & subject == counter
                                          & full_time_2 - full_time <= q95 
                                          & subject_2 == subject
                                          & actor != actor_2
                                          & subject_2 == counter
                                          & full_time_2 - full_time > 0
                                          & date2 == date,"yes","no"),time_to_opp = full_time_2 - full_time)
    } 
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    #combine dataframes into one huge dataframe
    testhuge <- bind_rows(opp_rand_test_2021, .id = "column_label")
    
    #sort by time and date
    testhugesorted <- testhuge %>%
      arrange(testhuge$date, testhuge$full_time)
    
    
    
    #need to check but i think it works to get rid of all "initiated no" rows to get rid of #duplicates, as opp 
    #is literally possible whenever an aggressive event takes place
    #Update. It does work
    
    testhugesorted_no_dup <- subset(testhugesorted, initiated != "no") 
    
    opp_no_dup_rand_2021 <- subset(testhugesorted_no_dup, select = c(date, full_time, actor, actor_rank, subject, subject_rank, bin, behavior, type, phase, counter, actor_2, actor_2_rank, subject_2, subject_2_rank,  full_time_2, date2, q95, initiated, rule_followed, time_to_opp))
    
    
    
    
    
    
    #end of opp process
    #end of rule code
    
    
    
    #bind vector of run IDs for each run to the dataframe so that you can have a numeric run #id next to each data point
    #that coresponds with that run which will allow you to sort/put them in ascending order later
    runIDs <- rep(loop.run, length(opp_no_dup_rand_2021$actor))
    
    opp_complete <- cbind(runIDs, opp_no_dup_rand_2021)
    
    
    #put each runs data into teh masterframe
    masterframe_opp_2021.st_unst.df <- rbind(masterframe_opp_2021.st_unst.df, opp_complete)
    
  }
  opp_count_length_list_masterframe_2021[[paste0("bin", k)]] <- masterframe_opp_2021.st_unst.df
}

opp_count_length_list_masterframe_2021_binded <-  bind_rows(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_2021, .id = "column_label")


end_time <- format(Sys.time(), "%H:%M:%S")
start_time
end_time


#write.csv(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_2021_binded,  here("ch3 data","opp_countmaster_stable_unstable_2021_custom_thresh.csv"), row.names = FALSE)



```

```{r}


##opp_count_length_list_masterframe_2021_binded <- read_csv(here("ch3 data","opp_countmaster_stable_unstable_2021_custom_thresh.csv"))


head(opp_doner_all_yes_2021) 



opp_count_length_sum_2021 <- opp_doner_all_yes_2021 %>% group_by( bin) %>%
  dplyr::summarize (
    count =n())

opp_count_length_sum_2021



```

#bin1
```{r}
#bin1

opp_count_length_list_masterframe_2021_binded_bin1 <- subset(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_2021_binded, bin == 1)

opp_count_length_list_2021_bin1_real <- subset(opp_count_length_sum_2021, bin == 1)




#put run #s in an ascending vector
cum_run_IDS <- unique(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_2021_binded_bin1$runIDs)

#create empty vector for number of time the rules were followed to be stored
random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 <- c()


#put the number of times the rule was followed into the vector for analysis 
for (j in cum_run_IDS) {
  len_of_opps <- length(which(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_2021_binded_bin1$rule_followed == "yes" & 
                                       opp_count_length_list_masterframe_2021_binded_bin1$runIDs == j))
  
  random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 <- c(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021, len_of_opps)
}

#turn vector into a dataframe for analysis
rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021 <- data.frame(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

#plot time (the x intercept is the # of time a rule was used in the real data, found with 
#"opp_real <- length(which(opp_no_dupr$rule_followed == "yes"))
#opp_real.frame <- data.frame(opp_real))"
st_unst_opp_2021_count_plot_bin1 <- ggplot(rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021, aes(x=random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=5,
                 colour="black", fill="grey") +
  geom_density(lwd = 1, linetype = 1) + theme_minimal() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = opp_count_length_list_2021_bin1_real$count, colour = "red", size = 2)  + theme(axis.text = element_text(size=25),  axis.title = element_blank()) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Count of opp events", breaks=seq(0, 200, 50)) +  
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 200), ylim = c(0,.05)) +
  scale_y_continuous( breaks = seq(0, .05, .025)) 

st_unst_opp_2021_count_plot_bin1

st_unst_opp_tally2021_bin1 <-length(which(opp_count_length_list_2021_bin1_real$count < rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021$random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 ))



st_unst_popp_tally2021_bin1 <-   1 - st_unst_opp_tally2021_bin1/length(rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021$random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

st_unst_popp_tally2021_bin1
 # 0 left




#calculate z score
#calculate mean and SD of permuted counts
mean_perm <- mean(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)
sd_perm <- sd(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

# Calculate z-score
z_score_bin1 <- (opp_count_length_list_2021_bin1_real$count - mean_perm) / sd_perm


#create df to store important data for later stats and figures. to be binded later
st_unst_df_piece_bin1 <- data.frame(group = integer(),
                                    bin = integer(),
                                    rule = character(),
                                    cmls = character(),
                                    p_cmls = integer(),
                                    z_score= integer(),
                                    smls = character(),
                                    stability = character(),
                                    treatment = character(),
                                    rank_4_treatment = character(),
                                    pre_post = character(),
                                    disturbanceID = character()
                                    )

#input data to be combined into df
new_row <- data.frame(
  group = 1,
  bin = 1,
  rule = "opp",
  cmls = "less",
  p_cmls = 0,
  z_score = z_score_bin1,
  smls = "slower",
  stability = "stable",
  treatment = "before",
  rank_4_treatment = "none",
  pre_post = "pre",
  disturbanceID = "removal1", 
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
#bind row of data to df
st_unst_df_piece_bin1 <- rbind(st_unst_df_piece_bin1, new_row)






st_unst_popp_tally2021_bin1.df <- data.frame(bin = 1, p_value = st_unst_popp_tally2021_bin1  )
```

```{r}
  geom_vline(xintercept = opp_count_length_list_2021_bin6_real$count, colour = "red", size = 2)  + theme(axis.text = element_text(size=25),  axis.title = element_blank()) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Count of opp events", breaks=seq(0, 200, 50)) +  
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 200), ylim = c(0,.05)) +
  scale_y_continuous( breaks = seq(0, .05, .025)) 

st_unst_opp_2021_count_plot_bin6

st_unst_opp_tally2021_bin6 <-length(which(opp_count_length_list_2021_bin6_real$count >rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021$random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 ))



st_unst_popp_tally2021_bin6 <-   1 - st_unst_opp_tally2021_bin6/length(rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021$random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

st_unst_popp_tally2021_bin6
 #p = 0 left



#calculate z score
#calculate mean and SD of permuted counts
mean_perm <- mean(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)
sd_perm <- sd(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

# Calculate z-score
z_score_bin6 <- (opp_count_length_list_2021_bin6_real$count - mean_perm) / sd_perm


#create df to store important data for later stats and figures. to be binded later
st_unst_df_piece_bin6 <- data.frame(group = integer(),
                                    bin = integer(),
                                    rule = character(),
                                    cmls = character(),
                                    p_cmls = integer(),
                                    z_score= integer(),
                                    smls = character(),
                                    stability = character(),
                                    treatment = character(),
                                    rank_4_treatment = character(),
                                    pre_post = character(),
                                    disturbanceID = character()
                                    )

#input data to be combined into df
new_row <- data.frame(
  group = 1,
  bin = 6,
  rule = "opp",
  cmls = "less",
  p_cmls = 0,
  z_score = z_score_bin6,
  smls = "slower",
  stability = "unstable",
  treatment = "removal",
  rank_4_treatment = "high",
  pre_post = "post",
  disturbanceID = "removal2", 
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
#bind row of data to df
st_unst_df_piece_bin6 <- rbind(st_unst_df_piece_bin6, new_row)




st_unst_popp_tally2021_bin6.df <- data.frame(bin = 6, p_value = st_unst_popp_tally2021_bin6  )
```



#bin7
```{r}
#bin7

opp_count_length_list_masterframe_2021_binded_bin7 <- subset(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_2021_binded, bin == 7)

opp_count_length_list_2021_bin7_real <- subset(opp_count_length_sum_2021, bin == 7)




#put run #s in an ascending vector
cum_run_IDS <- unique(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_2021_binded_bin7$runIDs)

#create empty vector for number of time the rules were followed to be stored
random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 <- c()


#put the number of times the rule was followed into the vector for analysis 
for (j in cum_run_IDS) {
  len_of_opps <- length(which(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_2021_binded_bin7$rule_followed == "yes" & 
                                       opp_count_length_list_masterframe_2021_binded_bin7$runIDs == j))
  
  random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 <- c(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021, len_of_opps)
}

#turn vector into a dataframe for analysis
rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021 <- data.frame(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

#plot time (the x intercept is the # of time a rule was used in the real data, found with 
#"opp_real <- length(which(opp_no_dupr$rule_followed == "yes"))
#opp_real.frame <- data.frame(opp_real))"
st_unst_opp_2021_count_plot_bin7 <- ggplot(rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021, aes(x=random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=5,
                 colour="black", fill="grey") +
  geom_density(lwd = 1, linetype = 1) + theme_minimal() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = opp_count_length_list_2021_bin7_real$count, colour = "red", size = 2)  + theme(axis.text = element_text(size=25),  axis.title = element_blank()) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Count of opp events", breaks=seq(0, 200, 50)) +  
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 200), ylim = c(0,.05)) +
  scale_y_continuous( breaks = seq(0, .05, .025)) 

st_unst_opp_2021_count_plot_bin7

st_unst_opp_tally2021_bin7 <-length(which( opp_count_length_list_2021_bin7_real$count >rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021$random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 ))



st_unst_popp_tally2021_bin7 <-   1 - st_unst_opp_tally2021_bin7/length(rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021$random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)
# 0 left
st_unst_popp_tally2021_bin7
 




#calculate z score
#calculate mean and SD of permuted counts
mean_perm <- mean(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)
sd_perm <- sd(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

# Calculate z-score
z_score_bin7 <- (opp_count_length_list_2021_bin7_real$count - mean_perm) / sd_perm


#create df to store important data for later stats and figures. to be binded later
st_unst_df_piece_bin7 <- data.frame(group = integer(),
                                    bin = integer(),
                                    rule = character(),
                                    cmls = character(),
                                    p_cmls = integer(),
                                    z_score= integer(),
                                    smls = character(),
                                    stability = character(),
                                    treatment = character(),
                                    rank_4_treatment = character(),
                                    pre_post = character(),
                                    disturbanceID = character()
                                    )

#input data to be combined into df
new_row <- data.frame(
  group = 1,
  bin = 7,
  rule = "opp",
  cmls = "less",
  p_cmls = 0,
  z_score = z_score_bin7,
  smls = "same" ,
  stability = "stable",
  treatment = "removal",
  rank_4_treatment = "high",
  pre_post = "pre",
  disturbanceID = "reintroduction2", 
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
#bind row of data to df
st_unst_df_piece_bin7 <- rbind(st_unst_df_piece_bin7, new_row)





st_unst_popp_tally2021_bin7.df <- data.frame(bin = 7, p_value = st_unst_popp_tally2021_bin7  )
```

```{r}
#put the number of times the rule was followed into the vector for analysis 
for (j in cum_run_IDS) {
  len_of_opps <- length(which(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_2021_binded_bin12$rule_followed == "yes" & 
                                       opp_count_length_list_masterframe_2021_binded_bin12$runIDs == j))
  
  random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 <- c(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021, len_of_opps)
}

#turn vector into a dataframe for analysis
rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021 <- data.frame(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

#plot time (the x intercept is the # of time a rule was used in the real data, found with 
#"opp_real <- length(which(opp_no_dupr$rule_followed == "yes"))
#opp_real.frame <- data.frame(opp_real))"
st_unst_opp_2021_count_plot_bin12 <- ggplot(rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021, aes(x=random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=5,
                 colour="black", fill="grey") +
  geom_density(lwd = 1, linetype = 1) + theme_minimal() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = opp_count_length_list_2021_bin12_real$count, colour = "red", size = 2)  + theme(axis.text = element_text(size=25),  axis.title = element_blank()) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Count of opp events", breaks=seq(0, 200, 50)) +  
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 200), ylim = c(0,.05)) +
  scale_y_continuous( breaks = seq(0, .05, .025)) 

st_unst_opp_2021_count_plot_bin12

st_unst_opp_tally2021_bin12 <-length(which(opp_count_length_list_2021_bin12_real$count > rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021$random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 ))



st_unst_popp_tally2021_bin12 <-   1 - st_unst_opp_tally2021_bin12/length(rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021$random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

st_unst_popp_tally2021_bin12
 # 0 left




#calculate z score
#calculate mean and SD of permuted counts
mean_perm <- mean(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)
sd_perm <- sd(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

# Calculate z-score
z_score_bin12 <- (opp_count_length_list_2021_bin12_real$count - mean_perm) / sd_perm


#create df to store important data for later stats and figures. to be binded later
st_unst_df_piece_bin12 <- data.frame(group = integer(),
                                    bin = integer(),
                                    rule = character(),
                                    cmls = character(),
                                    p_cmls = integer(),
                                    z_score= integer(),
                                    smls = character(),
                                    stability = character(),
                                    treatment = character(),
                                    rank_4_treatment = character(),
                                    pre_post = character(),
                                    disturbanceID = character()
                                    )

#input data to be combined into df
new_row <- data.frame(
  group = 1,
  bin = 12,
  rule = "opp",
  cmls = "less",
  p_cmls = 0 ,
  z_score = z_score_bin12,
  smls = "slower",
  stability = "unstable",
  treatment = "reintroduction",
  rank_4_treatment = "high",
  pre_post = "post",
  disturbanceID = "reintroduction3", 
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
#bind row of data to df
st_unst_df_piece_bin12 <- rbind(st_unst_df_piece_bin12, new_row)




st_unst_popp_tally2021_bin12.df <- data.frame(bin = 12, p_value = st_unst_popp_tally2021_bin12  )
```

#combine to make piece of masterframe
```{r}
#bind all summary dfs to make group masterframe
st_unst_df_piece_master_piece_opp_2021 <- rbind(st_unst_df_piece_bin1,  st_unst_df_piece_bin2, st_unst_df_piece_bin3, st_unst_df_piece_bin4, st_unst_df_piece_bin5, st_unst_df_piece_bin6, st_unst_df_piece_bin7, st_unst_df_piece_bin8, st_unst_df_piece_bin9, st_unst_df_piece_bin10, st_unst_df_piece_bin11, st_unst_df_piece_bin12)


#write.csv(st_unst_df_piece_master_piece_opp_2021,  here("ch3 data","st_unst_df_piece_master_piece_opp_2021.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
```

#randomize rule speed
```{r}
start_time <- format(Sys.time(), "%H:%M:%S")



dateact1 <-  c(1:length(opp_side_by_side))

#rename df for calculation of time to opp in seconds
opp_side_by_side_time_2021 <- opp_side_by_side


#pick out opp along rule while keeping all dataframes in a list (yay!!!)
for(i in dateact1 ) {
  opp_side_by_side_time_2021[[i]] <- mutate(opp_side_by_side_time_2021[[i]], initiated = ifelse(subject == counter
                                           & subject_2 == counter
                                           & subject_2_rank < actor_2_rank
                                           & actor != actor_2
                                           & subject == subject_2, "yes","no"), 
                                         rule_followed = ifelse( subject_2_rank < actor_2_rank
                                          & subject == counter
                                          & subject_2 == subject
                                          & actor != actor_2
                                          & subject_2 == counter
                                          & full_time_2 - full_time > 0
                                          & date2 == date,"yes","no"),time_to_opp = full_time_2 - full_time)
                                           
}




 #combine dataframes into one huge dataframe
 opp_time_huge_2021 <- bind_rows(opp_side_by_side_time_2021, .id = "column_label")
 
 

#Remove duplicate and double counted sequences
opp_side_by_side_time_2021_marked <- distinct(opp_time_huge_2021, date, full_time, actor, actor_rank, subject, subject_rank, obs_period, date_period, actor_2, actor_2_rank, subject_2, subject_2_rank, full_time_2, date2, time_to_opp, bin, behavior, type, phase,  .keep_all= TRUE) 







#remove instances of rule use whos speed were calculated to be 0. In this case we cant be certain of which event actually came first
opp_side_by_side_time_2021_marked <- filter(opp_side_by_side_time_2021_marked, time_to_opp != 0)



opp_obs_bin_2021 <- unique(opp_side_by_side_time_2021_marked$bin)
opp_time_length_list_masterframe_2021 <- list()

pbouter = txtProgressBar(min=0, max = length(opp_obs_bin_2021), style = 3)

set.seed(300)

for(k in opp_obs_bin_2021) { 
  runouter <- k
  setTxtProgressBar(pbouter, k) #update progress bar
  loop.run_outer <- paste("outer run", runouter) 
  loop.run_outer
  
  opp_filt_binded_2021_rand <- subset(opp_side_by_side_time_2021_marked, bin == k )  
  
  
  date_x <- opp_filt_binded_2021_rand$date
  obs <- opp_filt_binded_2021_rand$date_period
  #separate data into a list with each df=a date so we can randomize within days
  #sort_by_date <- function(x, i, y) {y <- filter(x, date == i )
  #mutate(y, date_counter = i)}
  
  opp_side_by_side_tested_time_2021_list <- list()
  dater <- unique(obs)
  datex <- c(1: length(unique(obs)))
  #boop <- filter(both_numbered_sorted, both_numbered_sorted$date == datex [2])
  
  #loop to create a separate dataframe for each bird that only contains interactions #pertaining to that bird.
  #Each dataframe is stored within a list to make the dataframes easy to work with
  #separate data into list of each obs session each day 
  for(i in datex) { 
    output <- filter(opp_filt_binded_2021_rand, opp_filt_binded_2021_rand$date_period == dater[i] )
    if (nrow(output) < 1) {output [ nrow(output) + 1 , ] <- NA
    } 
    opp_side_by_side_tested_time_2021_list[[paste0("list", dater[i])]] <- output}
  
  
  

nruns <- 1000

pb = txtProgressBar(min=0, max = nruns, style = 3)

#run ids should be 1 through however many times you want to run through the permutation
runIDs <- seq(1,1000)

#create an empty masterframe to be filled with each run of the permutation
masterframe_opp.df_time_2021 <- data.frame(runIDs = character(),
                             actor = character(),
                             actor_rank = numeric(),
                             subject = character(),
                             subject_rank = numeric(),
                             date = character(),
                             full_time = integer(),
                             bin = integer(),
                             behavior = character(),
                             type = character(),                                        
                             phase = character(),
                             counter = character(),
                             actor_2 = character(),
                             actor_2_rank = numeric(),
                             subject_2 = character(),
                             subject_2_rank = numeric(),
                             full_time_2 = integer(),
                             date2 = character(),
                             initiated = character(),
                             rule_followed = character(),
                             time_to_opp = integer(),
                            date_period = character(),
                             stringsAsFactors=FALSE)


set.seed(18)
#forloop starts here'
for (r in 1:length(runIDs)) { #r = 1 is a test you can uncomment to see if it works before #running it 100 times
#r = 1
  run <- r
      setTxtProgressBar(pb, r) #update progress bar
  loop.run <- paste("run", run) 
  loop.run
  #for example, if r = 1, looprun will say run 1 which will allow you to keep each run's #data straight
  
    

  
  #renamed data
  loop.datalist <- opp_side_by_side_tested_time_2021_list
    head(loop.datalist[[3]])
    xorder <- c()
  listlength <- as.numeric(length(loop.datalist))
datex2 <- 1:listlength
  full_time_to_rule <-c()
  loop.data.randomized.time_to_rule.added <- c()
    
    

  for(i in datex2) {
         #store time
     full_time_to_rule[[i]] <- subset(loop.datalist[[i]], select = c(time_to_opp))     
     #remove time from each df in the list. randomizing the order of time wouldnt make sense
     loop.datalist[[i]] <- subset(loop.datalist[[i]], select = -c(time_to_opp))
      #create a randomized sequence of numbers to bind to the subset data (ex. 2,6,1,3,5,4)
      #sort the data frame to put the randomized numbers in order from 1 to whatever and voila, the time between events (speed) has been  randomized (ex. from above 1,2,3,4,5,6)
    xorder[[i]] <- data.frame(xorder = sample(1:length(full_time_to_rule[[i]]$time_to_opp),
                                              length(full_time_to_rule[[i]]$time_to_opp)))
    #bind random order to df
    full_time_to_rule[[i]] <- mutate(full_time_to_rule[[i]], 
                                 xorderx = xorder[[i]]$xorder)
    #put randomized order of numbers in order to randomize speed in the dataset
    full_time_to_rule[[i]] <-
      full_time_to_rule[[i]][order(full_time_to_rule[[i]]$xorderx),]
    #bind time back on
    loop.data.randomized.time_to_rule.added[[i]] <- bind_cols(loop.datalist[[i]], full_time_to_rule[[i]])
    loop.data.randomized.time_to_rule.added[[i]] <- subset(loop.data.randomized.time_to_rule.added[[i]], select = -c(xorderx))

   }
  

  
  
  
  
  
  
  

#combine dataframes into one huge dataframe
loop.datalist_time_randomized_binded_tested_opp_2021 <- bind_rows(loop.data.randomized.time_to_rule.added, .id = "column_label")


 

#sort by time and date
loop.datalist_time_randomized_binded_tested_opp_2021 <- loop.datalist_time_randomized_binded_tested_opp_2021 %>%
  arrange(loop.datalist_time_randomized_binded_tested_opp_2021$date, loop.datalist_time_randomized_binded_tested_opp_2021$full_time)






#Remove duplicate events, events that have been represented twice, and events that could not confidently be counted as the start of opp
testhugesorted_no_dup_rand_time <- subset(loop.datalist_time_randomized_binded_tested_opp_2021, initiated != "no") 

opp_no_dup_rand_time <- subset(testhugesorted_no_dup_rand_time, select = c(date, full_time, actor, actor_rank, subject, subject_rank,
                                                               counter, bin, behavior, type, phase, actor_2, actor_2_rank, subject_2, subject_2_rank, full_time_2, date2, initiated,  rule_followed,time_to_opp, date_period))





  
  #end of opp process
  #end of rule code
  
  
  
  #bind vector of run IDs for each run to the dataframe so that you can have a numeric run #id next to each data point
  #that coresponds with that run which will allow you to sort/put them in ascending order later
 runIDs <- rep(loop.run, length(opp_no_dup_rand_time$actor))
 
  opp_complete_rand_time_2021 <- cbind(runIDs, opp_no_dup_rand_time)
  
  
#put each runs data into teh masterframe
  masterframe_opp.df_time_2021 <- rbind(masterframe_opp.df_time_2021, opp_complete_rand_time_2021)
  
}



opp_time_length_list_masterframe_2021[[paste0("bin", k)]] <- masterframe_opp.df_time_2021
}

opp_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2021_binded <-  bind_rows(opp_time_length_list_masterframe_2021, .id = "column_label")


end_time <- format(Sys.time(), "%H:%M:%S")
start_time
end_time



#write.csv(opp_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2021_binded,  here("ch3 data","opp_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2021_binded.csv"), row.names = FALSE)






```



#load df
```{r}


#opp_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2021_binded <- read_csv(here("ch3 data","opp_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2021_binded.csv"))


head(opp_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2021_binded) 


```

#bin1
```{r}
#bin1

opp_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2021_binded_bin1 <- subset(opp_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2021_binded, bin == 1)

opp_time_length_st_unst_2021_real_bin1 <- subset(opp_time_length_st_unst_2021, bin == 1)



#subset masterframe so that only rule follows are included
opp_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2021_only_follows_bin1 <- subset(opp_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2021_binded_bin1, rule_followed == "yes" )

#isolate time it takes to carry out rule...
masterframe_opp_time_sub.df_2021_bin1 <- subset( opp_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2021_only_follows_bin1, select = c(date, full_time, actor, subject, 
                                                                         actor_2, subject_2, full_time_2, date2, 
                                                                         initiated, rule_followed, time_to_opp, date_period, bin, behavior, type, phase))
head(masterframe_opp_time_sub.df_2021_bin1)



#add data label for the randomized rule speed
masterframe_opp_time_ready.df_bin1 <- mutate(masterframe_opp_time_sub.df_2021_bin1, data_type = "randomized")

#ensure that time is numeric
masterframe_opp_time_ready.df_bin1$time_to_opp <- as.numeric(masterframe_opp_time_ready.df_bin1$time_to_opp)


opp_time_length_st_unst_2021_real_bin1 <- subset( opp_time_length_st_unst_2021_real_bin1, select = c(date, full_time, actor, subject, 
                                                                         actor_2, subject_2, full_time_2, date2, 
                                                                         initiated, rule_followed, time_to_opp, date_period, bin, behavior, type, phase))



#add data label for the observed rule speed
opp_length_ready_2021_label_bin1 <- mutate(opp_time_length_st_unst_2021_real_bin1, data_type = "observed")



#combine df for observed rule speed and randomized rule speed into one df
opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin1 <- rbind(masterframe_opp_time_ready.df_bin1, opp_length_ready_2021_label_bin1 )
unique(opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin1$data_type)




#provide label for rule in question
opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin1 <- mutate(opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin1, rule = "opp")


#make sure that rule speed is numeric
opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin1$time_to_opp <- as.numeric(opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin1$time_to_opp)


#observed vs randomized raw plot. quick look at how the distriubtuions compare
ggplot(opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin1, aes(x=time_to_opp, fill = data_type)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=1) +#,
                 #colour="black", fill="white") + 
  geom_density() +   scale_x_continuous(name = "length in s of opp events random vs observed", limits = c(0,300)) 


#obtain mode of observed and randomized rule speed for plotting purposes
#getmode <- function(v) {
   #uniqv <- unique(v)
   #uniqv[which.max(tabulate(match(v, uniqv)))]
#}

#opp_2021_mode_obs <- getmode(opp_length_ready_2021_label$time_to_opp)

#opp_2021_mode_rand <-getmode(masterframe_opp_time_ready.df$time_to_opp)

#bind mode to dfs
#opp_2021_mode <-  rbind(opp_2021_mode_obs,opp_2021_mode_rand)

#test for homogeneity of variance
#leveneTest(time_to_opp ~ data_type, data = opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin1)


#split violin plot of observed vs randomized rule speed
opp_violin_plot_bin1 <- ggplot(opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin1, aes(rule, time_to_opp, fill = data_type))  +
  introdataviz::geom_split_violin(alpha= .9, trim = TRUE, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_boxplot(width = .2, alpha = .2, fatten = 2, show.legend = FALSE, outlier.shape = NA) +
  #stat_summary(fun = "mean", geom = "point",  colour = "darkblue", show.legend = F, 
               #position = position_dodge(.200))  +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Time Dependent Rule") +  
     coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1000)) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Speed of opp events (s)",
                     breaks = seq(0, 1000, 200) 
                     ) + scale_fill_manual(values=c("red","gray")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20)  + theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size=25), axis.title= element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank())



opp_violin_plot_bin1

#calculate the means for observed and randomized rule speed
mean_obs_rand_opp_2021 <- opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin1 %>% group_by(data_type) %>%
  dplyr::summarize (mean(time_to_opp))

mean_obs_rand_opp_2021
#obs 
#rand 


#linear mixed model to compare means of observed vs randomized rule speed

m1opp_bin1 <- glmer(time_to_opp ~ data_type  + (1 | date_period)  , data = opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin1 ,   control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun=2e5)), family = Gamma(link = "log"))
summary(m1opp_bin1)
#



#calculate confidence intervals  
confint_opp_2021_bin1 <- round(confint(m1opp_bin1, method = "Wald", level = 0.95), 3)
confint_opp_2021_bin1
#(Intercept)          
#data_typerandomized 


#(Intercept)         
#data_typerandomized 
#tukey
summary(glht(m1opp_bin1, linfct = mcp(data_type="Tukey")), test = adjusted("none"))
library(MuMIn)
#find R2 value
r.squaredGLMM(m1opp_bin1)
#


```

```{r}
#linear mixed model to compare means of observed vs randomized rule speed

#p = 9.74e-09


#calculate confidence intervals  
confint_opp_2021_bin2 <- round(confint(m1opp_bin2, method = "Wald", level = 0.95), 3)
confint_opp_2021_bin2

#(Intercept)          6.393  6.647
#data_typerandomized -0.395 -0.194


#tukey
summary(glht(m1opp_bin2, linfct = mcp(data_type="Tukey")), test = adjusted("none"))
library(MuMIn)
#find R2 value
r.squaredGLMM(m1opp_bin2)
#delta     2.871905e-05 0.06045786


```




#bin3
```{r}
#bin3

opp_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2021_binded_bin3 <- subset(opp_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2021_binded, bin == 3)

opp_time_length_st_unst_2021_real_bin3 <- subset(opp_time_length_st_unst_2021, bin == 3)



#subset masterframe so that only rule follows are included
opp_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2021_only_follows_bin3 <- subset(opp_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2021_binded_bin3, rule_followed == "yes" )

#isolate time it takes to carry out rule...
masterframe_opp_time_sub.df_2021_bin3 <- subset( opp_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2021_only_follows_bin3, select = c(date, full_time, actor, subject, 
                                                                         actor_2, subject_2, full_time_2, date2, 
                                                                         initiated, rule_followed, time_to_opp, date_period, bin, behavior, type, phase))
head(masterframe_opp_time_sub.df_2021_bin3)



#add data label for the randomized rule speed
masterframe_opp_time_ready.df_bin3 <- mutate(masterframe_opp_time_sub.df_2021_bin3, data_type = "randomized")

#ensure that time is numeric
masterframe_opp_time_ready.df_bin3$time_to_opp <- as.numeric(masterframe_opp_time_ready.df_bin3$time_to_opp)


opp_time_length_st_unst_2021_real_bin3 <- subset( opp_time_length_st_unst_2021_real_bin3, select = c(date, full_time, actor, subject, 
                                                                         actor_2, subject_2, full_time_2, date2, 
                                                                         initiated, rule_followed, time_to_opp, date_period, bin, behavior, type, phase))


#add data label for the observed rule speed
opp_length_ready_2021_label_bin3 <- mutate(opp_time_length_st_unst_2021_real_bin3, data_type = "observed")


#combine df for observed rule speed and randomized rule speed into one df
opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin3 <- rbind(masterframe_opp_time_ready.df_bin3, opp_length_ready_2021_label_bin3 )
unique(opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin3$data_type)




#provide label for rule in question
opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin3 <- mutate(opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin3, rule = "opp")


#make sure that rule speed is numeric
opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin3$time_to_opp <- as.numeric(opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin3$time_to_opp)


#observed vs randomized raw plot. quick look at how the distriubtuions compare
ggplot(opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin3, aes(x=time_to_opp, fill = data_type)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=1) +#,
                 #colour="black", fill="white") + 
  geom_density() +   scale_x_continuous(name = "length in s of opp events random vs observed", limits = c(0,300)) 


#obtain mode of observed and randomized rule speed for plotting purposes
#getmode <- function(v) {
   #uniqv <- unique(v)
   #uniqv[which.max(tabulate(match(v, uniqv)))]
#}

#opp_2021_mode_obs <- getmode(opp_length_ready_2021_label$time_to_opp)

#opp_2021_mode_rand <-getmode(masterframe_opp_time_ready.df$time_to_opp)

#bind mode to dfs
#opp_2021_mode <-  rbind(opp_2021_mode_obs,opp_2021_mode_rand)

#test for homogeneity of variance
#leveneTest(time_to_opp ~ data_type, data = opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin3)


#split violin plot of observed vs randomized rule speed
opp_violin_plot_bin3 <- ggplot(opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin3, aes(rule, time_to_opp, fill = data_type))  +
  introdataviz::geom_split_violin(alpha= .9, trim = TRUE, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_boxplot(width = .2, alpha = .2, fatten = 2, show.legend = FALSE, outlier.shape = NA) +
  #stat_summary(fun = "mean", geom = "point",  colour = "darkblue", show.legend = F, 
               #position = position_dodge(.200))  +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Time Dependent Rule") +  
     coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1000)) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Speed of opp events (s)",
                     breaks = seq(0, 1000, 200) 
                     ) + scale_fill_manual(values=c("red","gray")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20)  + theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size=25), axis.title= element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank())



opp_violin_plot_bin3

#calculate the means for observed and randomized rule speed
mean_obs_rand_opp_2021 <- opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin3 %>% group_by(data_type) %>%
  dplyr::summarize (mean(time_to_opp))

mean_obs_rand_opp_2021
#obs 727.4709
#rand 591.2071


#linear mixed model to compare means of observed vs randomized rule speed

m1opp_bin3 <- glmer(time_to_opp ~ data_type  + (1 | date_period)  , data = opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin3 ,   control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun=2e5)), family = Gamma(link = "log"))
summary(m1opp_bin3)
#p = 0.00149 


#calculate confidence intervals  
confint_opp_2021_bin3 <- round(confint(m1opp_bin3, method = "Wald", level = 0.95), 3)
confint_opp_2021_bin3
#(Intercept)          6.447  6.721
#data_typerandomized -0.328 -0.078

#tukey
summary(glht(m1opp_bin3, linfct = mcp(data_type="Tukey")), test = adjusted("none"))
library(MuMIn)
#find R2 value
r.squaredGLMM(m1opp_bin3)
#delta     1.536463e-05 0.010802268


```

```{r}
head(masterframe_opp_time_sub.df_2021_bin7)



#add data label for the randomized rule speed
masterframe_opp_time_ready.df_bin7 <- mutate(masterframe_opp_time_sub.df_2021_bin7, data_type = "randomized")

#ensure that time is numeric
masterframe_opp_time_ready.df_bin7$time_to_opp <- as.numeric(masterframe_opp_time_ready.df_bin7$time_to_opp)



opp_time_length_st_unst_2021_real_bin7 <- subset( opp_time_length_st_unst_2021_real_bin7, select = c(date, full_time, actor, subject, 
                                                                         actor_2, subject_2, full_time_2, date2, 
                                                                         initiated, rule_followed, time_to_opp, date_period, bin, behavior, type, phase))




#add data label for the observed rule speed
opp_length_ready_2021_label_bin7 <- mutate(opp_time_length_st_unst_2021_real_bin7, data_type = "observed")


#combine df for observed rule speed and randomized rule speed into one df
opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin7 <- rbind(masterframe_opp_time_ready.df_bin7, opp_length_ready_2021_label_bin7 )
unique(opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin7$data_type)




#provide label for rule in question
opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin7 <- mutate(opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin7, rule = "opp")


#make sure that rule speed is numeric
opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin7$time_to_opp <- as.numeric(opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin7$time_to_opp)


#observed vs randomized raw plot. quick look at how the distriubtuions compare
ggplot(opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin7, aes(x=time_to_opp, fill = data_type)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=1) +#,
                 #colour="black", fill="white") + 
  geom_density() +   scale_x_continuous(name = "length in s of opp events random vs observed", limits = c(0,300)) 


#obtain mode of observed and randomized rule speed for plotting purposes
#getmode <- function(v) {
   #uniqv <- unique(v)
   #uniqv[which.max(tabulate(match(v, uniqv)))]
#}

#opp_2021_mode_obs <- getmode(opp_length_ready_2021_label$time_to_opp)

#opp_2021_mode_rand <-getmode(masterframe_opp_time_ready.df$time_to_opp)

#bind mode to dfs
#opp_2021_mode <-  rbind(opp_2021_mode_obs,opp_2021_mode_rand)

#test for homogeneity of variance
#leveneTest(time_to_opp ~ data_type, data = opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin7)


#split violin plot of observed vs randomized rule speed
opp_violin_plot_bin7 <- ggplot(opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin7, aes(rule, time_to_opp, fill = data_type))  +
  introdataviz::geom_split_violin(alpha= .9, trim = TRUE, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_boxplot(width = .2, alpha = .2, fatten = 2, show.legend = FALSE, outlier.shape = NA) +
  #stat_summary(fun = "mean", geom = "point",  colour = "darkblue", show.legend = F, 
               #position = position_dodge(.200))  +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Time Dependent Rule") +  
     coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1000)) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Speed of opp events (s)",
                     breaks = seq(0, 1000, 200) 
                     ) + scale_fill_manual(values=c("red","gray")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20)  + theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size=25), axis.title= element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank())



opp_violin_plot_bin7

#calculate the means for observed and randomized rule speed
mean_obs_rand_opp_2021 <- opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin7 %>% group_by(data_type) %>%
  dplyr::summarize (mean(time_to_opp))

mean_obs_rand_opp_2021
#obs 543.168
#rand 436.490


#linear mixed model to compare means of observed vs randomized rule speed

m1opp_bin7 <- glmer(time_to_opp ~ data_type  + (1 | date_period) , data = opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin7 ,   control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun=2e5)), family = Gamma(link = "log"))
summary(m1opp_bin7)
#p = 0.000165 


#calculate confidence intervals  
confint_opp_2021_bin7 <- round(confint(m1opp_bin7, method = "Wald", level = 0.95), 3)
confint_opp_2021_bin7
#(Intercept)          6.182  6.459
#data_typerandomized -0.317 -0.100


#tukey
summary(glht(m1opp_bin7, linfct = mcp(data_type="Tukey")), test = adjusted("none"))
library(MuMIn)
#find R2 value
r.squaredGLMM(m1opp_bin7)
#delta     1.326699e-05 0.033498294


```

```{r}
opp_time_length_st_unst_2021_real_bin10 <- subset(opp_time_length_st_unst_2021, bin == 10)

#obs 931.8955

#data_typerandomized
```

```{r}
                                                                         actor_2, subject_2, full_time_2, date2, 
                                                                         initiated, rule_followed, time_to_opp, date_period, bin, behavior, type, phase))

#add data label for the randomized rule speed
masterframe_opp_time_ready.df_bin11 <- mutate(masterframe_opp_time_sub.df_2021_bin11, data_type = "randomized")

#ensure that time is numeric
```
