```{r}
opp_time_length_st_unst_2021_real_bin11 <- subset( opp_time_length_st_unst_2021_real_bin11, select = c(date, full_time, actor, subject, 
                                                                         actor_2, subject_2, full_time_2, date2, 
                                                                         initiated, rule_followed, time_to_opp, date_period, bin, behavior, type, phase))


opp_time_length_st_unst_2021_real_bin11 <- subset( opp_time_length_st_unst_2021_real_bin11, select = c(date, full_time, actor, subject, 
                                                                         actor_2, subject_2, full_time_2, date2, 
                                                                         initiated, rule_followed, time_to_opp, date_period, bin, behavior, type, phase))



#add data label for the observed rule speed
opp_length_ready_2021_label_bin11 <- mutate(opp_time_length_st_unst_2021_real_bin11, data_type = "observed")


#combine df for observed rule speed and randomized rule speed into one df
opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin11 <- rbind(masterframe_opp_time_ready.df_bin11, opp_length_ready_2021_label_bin11 )
unique(opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin11$data_type)




#provide label for rule in question
opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin11 <- mutate(opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin11, rule = "opp")


#make sure that rule speed is numeric
opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin11$time_to_opp <- as.numeric(opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin11$time_to_opp)


#observed vs randomized raw plot. quick look at how the distriubtuions compare
ggplot(opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin11, aes(x=time_to_opp, fill = data_type)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=1) +#,
                 #colour="black", fill="white") + 
  geom_density() +   scale_x_continuous(name = "length in s of opp events random vs observed", limits = c(0,300)) 


#obtain mode of observed and randomized rule speed for plotting purposes
#getmode <- function(v) {
   #uniqv <- unique(v)
   #uniqv[which.max(tabulate(match(v, uniqv)))]
#}

#opp_2021_mode_obs <- getmode(opp_length_ready_2021_label$time_to_opp)

#opp_2021_mode_rand <-getmode(masterframe_opp_time_ready.df$time_to_opp)

#bind mode to dfs
#opp_2021_mode <-  rbind(opp_2021_mode_obs,opp_2021_mode_rand)

#test for homogeneity of variance
#leveneTest(time_to_opp ~ data_type, data = opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin11)


#split violin plot of observed vs randomized rule speed
opp_violin_plot_bin11 <- ggplot(opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin11, aes(rule, time_to_opp, fill = data_type))  +
  introdataviz::geom_split_violin(alpha= .10, trim = TRUE, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_boxplot(width = .2, alpha = .2, fatten = 2, show.legend = FALSE, outlier.shape = NA) +
  #stat_summary(fun = "mean", geom = "point",  colour = "darkblue", show.legend = F, 
               #position = position_dodge(.200))  +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Time Dependent Rule") +  
     coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1000)) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Speed of opp events (s)",
                     breaks = seq(0, 1000, 200) 
                     ) + scale_fill_manual(values=c("red","gray")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20)  + theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size=25), axis.title= element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank())



opp_violin_plot_bin11

#calculate the means for observed and randomized rule speed
mean_obs_rand_opp_2021 <- opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin11 %>% group_by(data_type) %>%
  dplyr::summarize (mean(time_to_opp))

mean_obs_rand_opp_2021
#obs 575.3218
#rand 487.7558


#linear mixed model to compare means of observed vs randomized rule speed

m1opp_bin11 <- glmer(time_to_opp ~ data_type  + (1 | date_period)   , data = opp_length_ready_2021_comb_bin11 ,   control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun=2e5)), family = Gamma(link = "log"))
summary(m1opp_bin11)
#p = 0.0207 



#calculate confidence intervals  
confint_opp_2021_bin11 <- round(confint(m1opp_bin11, method = "Wald", level = 0.95), 3)
confint_opp_2021_bin11
#(Intercept)          6.313  6.607
#data_typerandomized -0.297 -0.025



#tukey
summary(glht(m1opp_bin11, linfct = mcp(data_type="Tukey")), test = adjusted("none"))
library(MuMIn)
#find R2 value
r.squaredGLMM(m1opp_bin11)
#delta     8.519356e-06 0.1018721


```

# day level randomize order of events
```{r}
#randomize order of events


#REMEMBER TO BIND THRESHOLD TO DF
start_time <- format(Sys.time(), "%H:%M:%S")



opp_obs_day_2021 <- unique(opp_filt_binded_2021_thresh$date)
opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day_2021 <- list()

pbouter = txtProgressBar(min=0, max = length(opp_obs_day_2021), style = 3)

set.seed(715)

for(k in opp_obs_day_2021) { 
  runouter <- k
  setTxtProgressBar(pbouter, k) #update progress bar
  loop.run_outer <- paste("outer run", runouter) 
  loop.run_outer
  
  opp_filt_binded_2021_rand <- subset(opp_filt_binded_2021_thresh, date == k )  
  #remove extra columns that create spam in loop
  opp_filt_binded_2021_rand <- subset(opp_filt_binded_2021_rand, select = c(date, full_time, actor, subject, bin, behavior, obs_period, date_period, type, phase, actor_rank, subject_rank, q95 ))
  
  
  obs <- opp_filt_binded_2021_rand$date_period
  #separate data into a list with each df=a date so we can randomize within days
  #sort_by_date <- function(x, i, y) {y <- filter(x, date == i )
  #mutate(y, date_counter = i)}
  
  datelist <- list()
  dater <- unique(obs)
  datex <- c(1: length(unique(obs)))
  #boop <- filter(both_numbered_sorted, both_numbered_sorted$date == datex [2])
  
  #loop to create a separate dataframe for each bird that only contains interactions #pertaining to that bird.
  #Each dataframe is stored within a list to make the dataframes easy to work with
  #separate data into list of each obs session each day 
  for(i in datex) { 
    output <- filter(opp_filt_binded_2021_rand, opp_filt_binded_2021_rand$date_period == dater[i] )
    if (nrow(output) < 1) {output [ nrow(output) + 1 , ] <- NA
    } 
    datelist[[paste0("list", dater[i])]] <- output}
  
  
  
  
  #run ids should be 1 through however many times you want to run through the permutation
  runIDs <- seq(1,100)
  
  #create an empty masterframe to be filled with each run of the permutation
  masterframe_day_opp_2021.st_unst.df <- data.frame(runIDs = character(),
                                                 actor = character(),
                                                 subject = character(),
                                                 date = character(),
                                                 full_time = integer(),
                                                 bin = integer(),
                                                 behavior = character(),
                                                 type = character(),
                                                 phase = character(),
                                                 actor_2 = character(),
                                                 subject_2 = character(),
                                                 full_time_2 = integer(),
                                                 date2 = character(),
                                                 q95 = numeric(),
                                                 counter = character(),
                                                 initiated = character(),
                                                 rule_followed = character(),
                                                 stringsAsFactors=FALSE)
  nruns <- 100
  #pb = txtProgressBar(min=0, max = nruns, style = 3)
  
  #forloop starts here'
  for (r in 1:length(runIDs)) { #r = 1 is a test you can uncomment to see if it works before #running it 100 times
    #r = 1
    run <- r
    #setTxtProgressBar(pb, r) #update progress bar
    loop.run <- paste("inner run", run) 
    loop.run
    #for example, if r = 1, looprun will say run 1 which will allow you to keep each run's #data straight
    
    
    #loop.seed <- set.seed[r] didnt work so ignore. not needed
    
    
    #renamed data
    loop.datalist <- datelist
    xorder <- c()
    listlength <- as.numeric(length(loop.datalist))
    datex2 <- 1:listlength
    full_time_list <-c()
    loop.data.randomized.time.added <- c()
    #get rid of anything thats not actor and receiver (we will bind back on the date and time #later anyways to preserve
    #their order)
    
    #remove date from each df in the list
    #create a randomized sequence of numbers to bind to the subset data (ex. 2,6,1,3,5,4)
    #sort the data frame to put the randomized numbers in order from 1 to whatever and voila, #the order of events has been randomized (ex. from above 1,2,3,4,5,6)
    
    for(i in datex2) {
      full_time_list[[i]] <- subset(loop.datalist[[i]], select = c(full_time))
      loop.datalist[[i]] <- subset(loop.datalist[[i]], select = -c(full_time))
      
      xorder[[i]] <- data.frame(xorder = sample(1:length(loop.datalist[[i]]$actor),
                                                length(loop.datalist[[i]]$actor)))
      loop.datalist[[i]] <- mutate(loop.datalist[[i]], 
                                   xorderx = xorder[[i]]$xorder)
      loop.datalist[[i]] <-
        loop.datalist[[i]][order(loop.datalist[[i]]$xorderx),]
      #bind time back on
      loop.data.randomized.time.added[[i]] <- bind_cols(loop.datalist[[i]], full_time_list[[i]])
      loop.data.randomized.time.added[[i]] <- subset(loop.data.randomized.time.added[[i]], select = -c(xorderx))
      
    }
    
    practdrlist <- loop.data.randomized.time.added
    
    

#separate data into list of each obs session each day for each bird

actex <- c(1:length(actorer))
dater <- unique(obs)
practdrlist_split_by_bird <- c()

for(i in datex2) {
for (g in actex) {output <- subset(practdrlist[[i]], actor == actorer[g] |  subject == actorer[g])
#if one of the dfs in the list is empty, add NA
   if (nrow(output) < 1) {output [ nrow(output) + 1 , ] <- NA
    } 
  output <- mutate(output, counter = actorer[g])
  practdrlist_split_by_bird[[paste0("list", dater[i], actorer[g])]] <- output
}}
    
    
    opp_2021_rand_split_by_bird_ordered <- lapply(practdrlist_split_by_bird, function(x) arrange(x, date, full_time))
    
    
    
    
    #modified each data frame to put consecutive events side by side
    
    opp_rand_test_2021 <- lapply(opp_2021_rand_split_by_bird_ordered, function(x) mutate(x, actor_2 = lead(actor), 
                                                                                   actor_2_rank = lead(actor_rank), 
                                                                                   subject_2 = lead(subject), 
                                                                                   subject_2_rank = lead(subject_rank), 
                                                                                   full_time_2 = lead(full_time), 
                                                                                   date2 = lead(date))) 
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    #successfully picked out opp rule while keeping all dataframes in a list (yay!!!)
    #loop to set conditionals to pick out opp
    
    #counter corresponds with which birds dataframe I am looking at and is used to control for false positives 
    #for example, if opp is detected for a bird whos specific dataframe I am not looking #at , this may be a false positive, as that birds interactions are not all listed in the #dataframe of another bird. In other words, the bird may have done something else to another #bird that is not listed, as it would be only listed in that specific birds dataframe
    dateact2 <-  c(1:length(opp_rand_test_2021))
    
    
    
    
    for(i in dateact2) {
      opp_rand_test_2021[[i]] <- mutate(opp_rand_test_2021[[i]], 
                                           initiated = ifelse(subject == counter
                                           , "yes","no"), 
                                         rule_followed = ifelse( subject_2_rank < actor_2_rank
                                          & subject == counter
                                          & full_time_2 - full_time <= q95 
                                          & subject_2 == subject
                                          & actor != actor_2
                                          & subject_2 == counter
                                          & full_time_2 - full_time > 0
                                          & date2 == date,"yes","no"),time_to_opp = full_time_2 - full_time)
    } 
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    #combine dataframes into one huge dataframe
    testhuge <- bind_rows(opp_rand_test_2021, .id = "column_label")
    
    #sort by time and date
    testhugesorted <- testhuge %>%
      arrange(testhuge$date, testhuge$full_time)
    
    
    
    #need to check but i think it works to get rid of all "initiated no" rows to get rid of #duplicates, as opp 
    #is literally possible whenever an aggressive event takes place
    #Update. It does work
    
    testhugesorted_no_dup <- subset(testhugesorted, initiated != "no") 
    
    
    opp_no_dup_rand_2021 <- subset(testhugesorted_no_dup, select = c(date, full_time, actor, actor_rank, subject, subject_rank, bin, behavior, type, phase, counter, actor_2, actor_2_rank, subject_2, subject_2_rank,  full_time_2, date2, q95, initiated, rule_followed, time_to_opp))
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    #end of opp process
    #end of rule code
    
    
    
    #bind vector of run IDs for each run to the dataframe so that you can have a numeric run #id next to each data point
    #that coresponds with that run which will allow you to sort/put them in ascending order later
    runIDs <- rep(loop.run, length(opp_no_dup_rand_2021$actor))
    
    opp_complete <- cbind(runIDs, opp_no_dup_rand_2021)
    
    
    #put each runs data into teh masterframe
    masterframe_day_opp_2021.st_unst.df <- rbind(masterframe_day_opp_2021.st_unst.df, opp_complete)
    
  }
  opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day_2021[[paste0("day", k)]] <- masterframe_day_opp_2021.st_unst.df
}

opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day_2021_binded <-  bind_rows(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day_2021, .id = "column_label")


end_time <- format(Sys.time(), "%H:%M:%S")
start_time
end_time


#write.csv(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day_2021_binded,  here("ch3 data","opp_countmaster_day_level_stable_unstable_2021_custom_thresh.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

```

```{r}


#opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day_2021_binded <- read_csv(here("ch3 data",("opp_countmaster_day_level_stable_unstable_2021_custom_thresh.csv")))
opp_doner_all_yes_2021

head(opp_doner_all_yes_2021) 



opp_count_length_sum_2021 <- opp_doner_all_yes_2021 %>% group_by( date) %>%
  dplyr::summarize (
    count =n())

opp_count_length_sum_2021


opp_dates_2021 <- unique(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day_2021_binded$date)
```



#day1
```{r}
#day1

current_date <- opp_dates_2021[1]
current_date



opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day_2021_binded_day1 <- subset(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day_2021_binded, date == current_date)

opp_count_length_list_2021_day1_real <- subset(opp_count_length_sum_2021, date == current_date)




#put run #s in an ascending vector
cum_run_IDS <- unique(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day_2021_binded_day1$runIDs)

#create empty vector for number of time the rules were followed to be stored
random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 <- c()


#put the number of times the rule was followed into the vector for analysis 
for (j in cum_run_IDS) {
  len_of_opps <- length(which(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day_2021_binded_day1$rule_followed == "yes" & 
                                       opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day_2021_binded_day1$runIDs == j))
  
  random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 <- c(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021, len_of_opps)
}

#turn vector into a dataframe for analysis
rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021 <- data.frame(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

#plot time (the x intercept is the # of time a rule was used in the real data, found with 
#"opp_real <- length(which(opp_no_dupr$rule_followed == "yes"))
#opp_real.frame <- data.frame(opp_real))"
st_unst_opp_2021_count_plot_day1 <- ggplot(rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021, aes(x=random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=5,
                 colour="black", fill="grey") +
  geom_density(lwd = 1, linetype = 1) + theme_minimal() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = opp_count_length_list_2021_day1_real$count, colour = "red", size = 2)  + theme(axis.text = element_text(size=25),  axis.title = element_blank()) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Count of opp events", breaks=seq(0, 200, 50)) +  
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 200), ylim = c(0,.05)) +
  scale_y_continuous( breaks = seq(0, .05, .025)) 

st_unst_opp_2021_count_plot_day1

st_unst_opp_tally2021_day1 <-length(which(opp_count_length_list_2021_day1_real$count < rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021$random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 ))



st_unst_popp_tally2021_day1 <-   1 - st_unst_opp_tally2021_day1/length(rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021$random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

st_unst_popp_tally2021_day1

#p = 
#calculate z score
#calculate mean and SD of permuted counts
mean_perm <- mean(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)
sd_perm <- sd(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

# Calculate z-score
z_score_day1 <- (opp_count_length_list_2021_day1_real$count - mean_perm) / sd_perm



#create df to store important data for later stats and figures. to be binded later
st_unst_df_piece_day1 <- data.frame(group = integer(),
                                    bin = integer(),
                                    date =  character(),
                                    rule = character(),
                                    cmls = character(),
                                    p_cmls = integer(),
                                    z_score= integer())

 bin_for_store <- unique(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day_2021_binded_day1$bin)



#input data to be combined into df
new_row <- data.frame(
  group = 1,
  bin = bin_for_store, date = current_date, 
  rule = "opp",
  cmls = "same",
  p_cmls = 0.27 ,
  z_score = z_score_day1,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
#bind row of data to df
st_unst_df_piece_day1 <- rbind(st_unst_df_piece_day1, new_row)

```

#day2
```{r}
#day2

current_date <- opp_dates_2021[2]
current_date



opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day2_2021_binded_day2 <- subset(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day_2021_binded, date == current_date)

opp_count_length_list_2021_day2_real <- subset(opp_count_length_sum_2021, date == current_date)




#put run #s in an ascending vector
cum_run_IDS <- unique(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day2_2021_binded_day2$runIDs)

#create empty vector for number of time the rules were followed to be stored
random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 <- c()


#put the number of times the rule was followed into the vector for analysis 
for (j in cum_run_IDS) {
  len_of_opps <- length(which(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day2_2021_binded_day2$rule_followed == "yes" & 
                                       opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day2_2021_binded_day2$runIDs == j))
  
  random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 <- c(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021, len_of_opps)
}

#turn vector into a dataframe for analysis
rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021 <- data.frame(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

#plot time (the x intercept is the # of time a rule was used in the real data, found with 
#"opp_real <- length(which(opp_no_dupr$rule_followed == "yes"))
#opp_real.frame <- data.frame(opp_real))"
st_unst_opp_2021_count_plot_day2 <- ggplot(rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021, aes(x=random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=5,
                 colour="black", fill="grey") +
  geom_density(lwd = 1, linetype = 1) + theme_minimal() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = opp_count_length_list_2021_day2_real$count, colour = "red", size = 2)  + theme(axis.text = element_text(size=25),  axis.title = element_blank()) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Count of opp events", breaks=seq(0, 200, 50)) +  
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 200), ylim = c(0,.05)) +
  scale_y_continuous( breaks = seq(0, .05, .025)) 

st_unst_opp_2021_count_plot_day2

st_unst_opp_tally2021_day2 <-length(which(opp_count_length_list_2021_day2_real$count > rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021$random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 ))



st_unst_popp_tally2021_day2 <-   1 - st_unst_opp_tally2021_day2/length(rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021$random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

st_unst_popp_tally2021_day2

#p = 
#calculate z score
#calculate mean and SD of permuted counts
mean_perm <- mean(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)
sd_perm <- sd(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

# Calculate z-score
z_score_day2 <- (opp_count_length_list_2021_day2_real$count - mean_perm) / sd_perm


#create df to store important data for later stats and figures. to be binded later
st_unst_df_piece_day2 <- data.frame(group = integer(),
                                    bin = integer(),
                                    date =  character(),
                                    rule = character(),
                                    cmls = character(),
                                    p_cmls = integer(),
                                    z_score= integer())

 bin_for_store <- unique(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day2_2021_binded_day2$bin)



#input data to be combined into df
new_row <- data.frame(
  group = 1,
  bin = bin_for_store, date = current_date, 
  rule = "opp",
  cmls = "same",
  p_cmls = 0.34 ,
  z_score = z_score_day2,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
#bind row of data to df
st_unst_df_piece_day2 <- rbind(st_unst_df_piece_day2, new_row)

```

```{r}
current_date <- opp_dates_2021[8]
current_date



opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day8_2021_binded_day8 <- subset(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day_2021_binded, date == current_date)

opp_count_length_list_2021_day8_real <- subset(opp_count_length_sum_2021, date == current_date)




#put run #s in an ascending vector
cum_run_IDS <- unique(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day8_2021_binded_day8$runIDs)

#create empty vector for number of time the rules were followed to be stored
random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 <- c()


#put the number of times the rule was followed into the vector for analysis 
for (j in cum_run_IDS) {
  len_of_opps <- length(which(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day8_2021_binded_day8$rule_followed == "yes" & 
                                       opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day8_2021_binded_day8$runIDs == j))
  
  random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 <- c(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021, len_of_opps)
}

#turn vector into a dataframe for analysis
rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021 <- data.frame(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

#plot time (the x intercept is the # of time a rule was used in the real data, found with 
#"opp_real <- length(which(opp_no_dupr$rule_followed == "yes"))
#opp_real.frame <- data.frame(opp_real))"
st_unst_opp_2021_count_plot_day8 <- ggplot(rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021, aes(x=random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=5,
                 colour="black", fill="grey") +
  geom_density(lwd = 1, linetype = 1) + theme_minimal() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = opp_count_length_list_2021_day8_real$count, colour = "red", size = 2)  + theme(axis.text = element_text(size=25),  axis.title = element_blank()) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Count of opp events", breaks=seq(0, 200, 50)) +  
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 200), ylim = c(0,.05)) +
  scale_y_continuous( breaks = seq(0, .05, .025)) 

st_unst_opp_2021_count_plot_day8

st_unst_opp_tally2021_day8 <-length(which(opp_count_length_list_2021_day8_real$count > rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021$random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 ))



st_unst_popp_tally2021_day8 <-   1 - st_unst_opp_tally2021_day8/length(rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021$random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

st_unst_popp_tally2021_day8

#p = 
#calculate z score
#calculate mean and SD of permuted counts
mean_perm <- mean(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)
sd_perm <- sd(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

# Calculate z-score
z_score_day8 <- (opp_count_length_list_2021_day8_real$count - mean_perm) / sd_perm


#create df to store important data for later stats and figures. to be binded later
st_unst_df_piece_day8 <- data.frame(group = integer(),
                                    bin = integer(),
                                    date =  character(),
                                    rule = character(),
                                    cmls = character(),
                                    p_cmls = integer(),
                                    z_score= integer())

 bin_for_store <- unique(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day8_2021_binded_day8$bin)



#input data to be combined into df
new_row <- data.frame(
  group = 1,
  bin = bin_for_store, date = current_date,
  rule = "opp",
  cmls = "same",
  p_cmls = 0.18 ,
  z_score = z_score_day8,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
#bind row of data to df
st_unst_df_piece_day8 <- rbind(st_unst_df_piece_day8, new_row)

```

```{r}
#put run #s in an ascending vector
cum_run_IDS <- unique(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day14_2021_binded_day14$runIDs)

#create empty vector for number of time the rules were followed to be stored
random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 <- c()


#put the number of times the rule was followed into the vector for analysis 
for (j in cum_run_IDS) {
  len_of_opps <- length(which(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day14_2021_binded_day14$rule_followed == "yes" & 
                                       opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day14_2021_binded_day14$runIDs == j))
  
  random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 <- c(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021, len_of_opps)
}

#turn vector into a dataframe for analysis
rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021 <- data.frame(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

#plot time (the x intercept is the # of time a rule was used in the real data, found with 
#"opp_real <- length(which(opp_no_dupr$rule_followed == "yes"))
#opp_real.frame <- data.frame(opp_real))"
st_unst_opp_2021_count_plot_day14 <- ggplot(rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021, aes(x=random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=5,
                 colour="black", fill="grey") +
  geom_density(lwd = 1, linetype = 1) + theme_minimal() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = opp_count_length_list_2021_day14_real$count, colour = "red", size = 2)  + theme(axis.text = element_text(size=25),  axis.title = element_blank()) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Count of opp events", breaks=seq(0, 200, 50)) +  
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 200), ylim = c(0,.05)) +
  scale_y_continuous( breaks = seq(0, .05, .025)) 

st_unst_opp_2021_count_plot_day14

st_unst_opp_tally2021_day14 <-length(which(opp_count_length_list_2021_day14_real$count > rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021$random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 ))



st_unst_popp_tally2021_day14 <-   1 - st_unst_opp_tally2021_day14/length(rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021$random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

st_unst_popp_tally2021_day14

#p = 
#calculate z score
#calculate mean and SD of permuted counts
mean_perm <- mean(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)
sd_perm <- sd(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

# Calculate z-score
z_score_day14 <- (opp_count_length_list_2021_day14_real$count - mean_perm) / sd_perm


#create df to store important data for later stats and figures. to be binded later
st_unst_df_piece_day14 <- data.frame(group = integer(),
                                    bin = integer(),
                                    date =  character(),
                                    rule = character(),
                                    cmls = character(),
                                    p_cmls = integer(),
                                    z_score= integer())

 bin_for_store <- unique(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day14_2021_binded_day14$bin)



#input data to be combined into df
new_row <- data.frame(
  group = 1,
  bin = bin_for_store, date = current_date,
  rule = "opp",
  cmls = "same",
  p_cmls = 0.22 ,
  z_score = z_score_day14,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
#bind row of data to df
st_unst_df_piece_day14 <- rbind(st_unst_df_piece_day14, new_row)

```

```{r}
for (j in cum_run_IDS) {
  len_of_opps <- length(which(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day20_2021_binded_day20$rule_followed == "yes" & 
                                       opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day20_2021_binded_day20$runIDs == j))
  
  random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 <- c(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021, len_of_opps)
}

#turn vector into a dataframe for analysis
rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021 <- data.frame(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

#plot time (the x intercept is the # of time a rule was used in the real data, found with 
#"opp_real <- length(which(opp_no_dupr$rule_followed == "yes"))
#opp_real.frame <- data.frame(opp_real))"
st_unst_opp_2021_count_plot_day20 <- ggplot(rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021, aes(x=random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=5,
                 colour="black", fill="grey") +
  geom_density(lwd = 1, linetype = 1) + theme_minimal() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = opp_count_length_list_2021_day20_real$count, colour = "red", size = 2)  + theme(axis.text = element_text(size=25),  axis.title = element_blank()) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Count of opp events", breaks=seq(0, 200, 50)) +  
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 200), ylim = c(0,.05)) +
  scale_y_continuous( breaks = seq(0, .05, .025)) 

st_unst_opp_2021_count_plot_day20

st_unst_opp_tally2021_day20 <-length(which(opp_count_length_list_2021_day20_real$count > rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021$random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 ))



st_unst_popp_tally2021_day20 <-   1 - st_unst_opp_tally2021_day20/length(rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021$random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

st_unst_popp_tally2021_day20

#p = 
#calculate z score
#calculate mean and SD of permuted counts
mean_perm <- mean(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)
sd_perm <- sd(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

# Calculate z-score
z_score_day20 <- (opp_count_length_list_2021_day20_real$count - mean_perm) / sd_perm


#create df to store important data for later stats and figures. to be binded later
st_unst_df_piece_day20 <- data.frame(group = integer(),
                                    bin = integer(),
                                    date =  character(),
                                    rule = character(),
                                    cmls = character(),
                                    p_cmls = integer(),
                                    z_score= integer())

 bin_for_store <- unique(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day20_2021_binded_day20$bin)



#input data to be combined into df
new_row <- data.frame(
  group = 1,
  bin = bin_for_store, date = current_date,
  rule = "opp",
  cmls = "same",
  p_cmls = 0.38 ,
  z_score = z_score_day20,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
#bind row of data to df
st_unst_df_piece_day20 <- rbind(st_unst_df_piece_day20, new_row)

```

```{r}
rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021 <- data.frame(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

#plot time (the x intercept is the # of time a rule was used in the real data, found with 
#"opp_real <- length(which(opp_no_dupr$rule_followed == "yes"))
#opp_real.frame <- data.frame(opp_real))"
st_unst_opp_2021_count_plot_day26 <- ggplot(rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021, aes(x=random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=5,
                 colour="black", fill="grey") +
  geom_density(lwd = 1, linetype = 1) + theme_minimal() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = opp_count_length_list_2021_day26_real$count, colour = "red", size = 2)  + theme(axis.text = element_text(size=25),  axis.title = element_blank()) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Count of opp events", breaks=seq(0, 200, 50)) +  
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 200), ylim = c(0,.05)) +
  scale_y_continuous( breaks = seq(0, .05, .025)) 

st_unst_opp_2021_count_plot_day26

st_unst_opp_tally2021_day26 <-length(which(opp_count_length_list_2021_day26_real$count > rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021$random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 ))



st_unst_popp_tally2021_day26 <-   1 - st_unst_opp_tally2021_day26/length(rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021$random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

st_unst_popp_tally2021_day26

#p = 
#calculate z score
#calculate mean and SD of permuted counts
mean_perm <- mean(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)
sd_perm <- sd(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

# Calculate z-score
z_score_day26 <- (opp_count_length_list_2021_day26_real$count - mean_perm) / sd_perm


#create df to store important data for later stats and figures. to be binded later
st_unst_df_piece_day26 <- data.frame(group = integer(),
                                    bin = integer(),
                                    date =  character(),
                                    rule = character(),
                                    cmls = character(),
                                    p_cmls = integer(),
                                    z_score= integer())

 bin_for_store <- unique(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day26_2021_binded_day26$bin)



#input data to be combined into df
new_row <- data.frame(
  group = 1,
  bin = bin_for_store, date = current_date,
  rule = "opp",
  cmls = "same",
  p_cmls = 0.06 ,
  z_score = z_score_day26,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
#bind row of data to df
st_unst_df_piece_day26 <- rbind(st_unst_df_piece_day26, new_row)

```

```{r}
#"opp_real <- length(which(opp_no_dupr$rule_followed == "yes"))
#opp_real.frame <- data.frame(opp_real))"
st_unst_opp_2021_count_plot_day32 <- ggplot(rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021, aes(x=random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=5,
                 colour="black", fill="grey") +
  geom_density(lwd = 1, linetype = 1) + theme_minimal() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = opp_count_length_list_2021_day32_real$count, colour = "red", size = 2)  + theme(axis.text = element_text(size=25),  axis.title = element_blank()) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Count of opp events", breaks=seq(0, 200, 50)) +  
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 200), ylim = c(0,.05)) +
  scale_y_continuous( breaks = seq(0, .05, .025)) 

st_unst_opp_2021_count_plot_day32

st_unst_opp_tally2021_day32 <-length(which(opp_count_length_list_2021_day32_real$count < rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021$random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 ))



st_unst_popp_tally2021_day32 <-   1 - st_unst_opp_tally2021_day32/length(rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021$random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

st_unst_popp_tally2021_day32

#p = 
#calculate z score
#calculate mean and SD of permuted counts
mean_perm <- mean(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)
sd_perm <- sd(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

# Calculate z-score
z_score_day32 <- (opp_count_length_list_2021_day32_real$count - mean_perm) / sd_perm


#create df to store important data for later stats and figures. to be binded later
st_unst_df_piece_day32 <- data.frame(group = integer(),
                                    bin = integer(),
                                    date =  character(),
                                    rule = character(),
                                    cmls = character(),
                                    p_cmls = integer(),
                                    z_score= integer())

 bin_for_store <- unique(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day32_2021_binded_day32$bin)



#input data to be combined into df
new_row <- data.frame(
  group = 1,
  bin = bin_for_store, date = current_date,
  rule = "opp",
  cmls = "same",
  p_cmls = 0.27 ,
  z_score = z_score_day32,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
#bind row of data to df
st_unst_df_piece_day32 <- rbind(st_unst_df_piece_day32, new_row)

```

#day33

```{r}
current_date



opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day33_2021_binded_day33 <- subset(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day_2021_binded, date == current_date)

opp_count_length_list_2021_day33_real <- subset(opp_count_length_sum_2021, date == current_date)




#put run #s in an ascending vector
cum_run_IDS <- unique(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day33_2021_binded_day33$runIDs)

#create empty vector for number of time the rules were followed to be stored
random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 <- c()


#put the number of times the rule was followed into the vector for analysis 
for (j in cum_run_IDS) {
  len_of_opps <- length(which(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day33_2021_binded_day33$rule_followed == "yes" & 
                                       opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day33_2021_binded_day33$runIDs == j))
  
  random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 <- c(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021, len_of_opps)
}

#"opp_real <- length(which(opp_no_dupr$rule_followed == "yes"))

  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=5,
                 colour="black", fill="grey") +
  geom_density(lwd = 1, linetype = 1) + theme_minimal() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = opp_count_length_list_2021_day33_real$count, colour = "red", size = 2)  + theme(axis.text = element_text(size=25),  axis.title = element_blank()) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Count of opp events", breaks=seq(0, 200, 50)) +  
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 200), ylim = c(0,.05)) +
  scale_y_continuous( breaks = seq(0, .05, .025)) 

st_unst_opp_2021_count_plot_day33
```

```{r}
  geom_density(lwd = 1, linetype = 1) + theme_minimal() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = opp_count_length_list_2021_day38_real$count, colour = "red", size = 2)  + theme(axis.text = element_text(size=25),  axis.title = element_blank()) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Count of opp events", breaks=seq(0, 200, 50)) +  
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 200), ylim = c(0,.05)) +
  scale_y_continuous( breaks = seq(0, .05, .025)) 

st_unst_opp_2021_count_plot_day38

st_unst_opp_tally2021_day38 <-length(which(opp_count_length_list_2021_day38_real$count > rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021$random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 ))



st_unst_popp_tally2021_day38 <-   1 - st_unst_opp_tally2021_day38/length(rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021$random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

st_unst_popp_tally2021_day38

#p = 
#calculate z score
#calculate mean and SD of permuted counts
mean_perm <- mean(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)
sd_perm <- sd(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

# Calculate z-score
z_score_day38 <- (opp_count_length_list_2021_day38_real$count - mean_perm) / sd_perm


#create df to store important data for later stats and figures. to be binded later
st_unst_df_piece_day38 <- data.frame(group = integer(),
                                    bin = integer(),
                                    date =  character(),
                                    rule = character(),
                                    cmls = character(),
                                    p_cmls = integer(),
                                    z_score= integer())

 bin_for_store <- unique(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day38_2021_binded_day38$bin)



#input data to be combined into df
new_row <- data.frame(
  group = 1,
  bin = bin_for_store, date = current_date,
  rule = "opp",
  cmls = "same",
  p_cmls = 0.17 ,
  z_score = z_score_day38,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
#bind row of data to df
st_unst_df_piece_day38 <- rbind(st_unst_df_piece_day38, new_row)

```

#day39
```{r}
#day39

current_date <- opp_dates_2021[39]
current_date



opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day39_2021_binded_day39 <- subset(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day_2021_binded, date == current_date)

opp_count_length_list_2021_day39_real <- subset(opp_count_length_sum_2021, date == current_date)




#put run #s in an ascending vector
cum_run_IDS <- unique(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day39_2021_binded_day39$runIDs)

#create empty vector for number of time the rules were followed to be stored
random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 <- c()


#put the number of times the rule was followed into the vector for analysis 
for (j in cum_run_IDS) {
  len_of_opps <- length(which(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day39_2021_binded_day39$rule_followed == "yes" & 
                                       opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day39_2021_binded_day39$runIDs == j))
  
  random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 <- c(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021, len_of_opps)
}

#turn vector into a dataframe for analysis
rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021 <- data.frame(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

#plot time (the x intercept is the # of time a rule was used in the real data, found with 
#"opp_real <- length(which(opp_no_dupr$rule_followed == "yes"))
#opp_real.frame <- data.frame(opp_real))"
st_unst_opp_2021_count_plot_day39 <- ggplot(rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021, aes(x=random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=5,
                 colour="black", fill="grey") +
  geom_density(lwd = 1, linetype = 1) + theme_minimal() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = opp_count_length_list_2021_day39_real$count, colour = "red", size = 2)  + theme(axis.text = element_text(size=25),  axis.title = element_blank()) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Count of opp events", breaks=seq(0, 200, 50)) +  
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 200), ylim = c(0,.05)) +
  scale_y_continuous( breaks = seq(0, .05, .025)) 

st_unst_opp_2021_count_plot_day39

st_unst_opp_tally2021_day39 <-length(which(opp_count_length_list_2021_day39_real$count > rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021$random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 ))



st_unst_popp_tally2021_day39 <-   1 - st_unst_opp_tally2021_day39/length(rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021$random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

st_unst_popp_tally2021_day39

sd_perm <- sd(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

# Calculate z-score

                                    rule = character(),

                                    p_cmls = integer(),
                                    z_score= integer())

#input data to be combined into df

  group = 1,
  bin = bin_for_store, date = current_date,
  rule = "opp",
  cmls = "more",
```

```{r}
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Count of opp events", breaks=seq(0, 200, 50)) +  
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 200), ylim = c(0,.05)) +
  scale_y_continuous( breaks = seq(0, .05, .025)) 

st_unst_opp_2021_count_plot_day44

st_unst_opp_tally2021_day44 <-length(which(opp_count_length_list_2021_day44_real$count < rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021$random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021 ))



st_unst_popp_tally2021_day44 <-   1 - st_unst_opp_tally2021_day44/length(rand_opp_.frame_st_unst_2021$random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

st_unst_popp_tally2021_day44

#p = 
#calculate z score
#calculate mean and SD of permuted counts
mean_perm <- mean(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)
sd_perm <- sd(random_times_opp_followed_st_unst_2021)

# Calculate z-score
z_score_day44 <- (opp_count_length_list_2021_day44_real$count - mean_perm) / sd_perm


#create df to store important data for later stats and figures. to be binded later
st_unst_df_piece_day44 <- data.frame(group = integer(),
                                    bin = integer(),
                                    date =  character(),
                                    rule = character(),
                                    cmls = character(),
                                    p_cmls = integer(),
                                    z_score= integer())

 bin_for_store <- unique(opp_count_length_list_masterframe_day44_2021_binded_day44$bin)



#input data to be combined into df
new_row <- data.frame(
  group = 1,
  bin = bin_for_store, date = current_date,
  rule = "opp",
  cmls = "same",
  p_cmls = 0.42 ,
  z_score = z_score_day44,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
#bind row of data to df
st_unst_df_piece_day44 <- rbind(st_unst_df_piece_day44, new_row)

```






#combine dataframe
```{r}



#bind all summary dfs to make group masterframe
st_unst_df_piece_master_piece_opp_2021_day_by_day <- rbind(st_unst_df_piece_day1,  st_unst_df_piece_day2,  st_unst_df_piece_day3,  st_unst_df_piece_day4,  st_unst_df_piece_day5,  st_unst_df_piece_day6,  st_unst_df_piece_day7,  st_unst_df_piece_day8,  st_unst_df_piece_day9,  st_unst_df_piece_day10,  st_unst_df_piece_day11,  st_unst_df_piece_day12,  st_unst_df_piece_day13,  st_unst_df_piece_day14,  st_unst_df_piece_day15,  st_unst_df_piece_day16,  st_unst_df_piece_day17,  st_unst_df_piece_day18,  st_unst_df_piece_day19,  st_unst_df_piece_day20,  st_unst_df_piece_day21,  st_unst_df_piece_day22,  st_unst_df_piece_day23,  st_unst_df_piece_day24,  st_unst_df_piece_day25,  st_unst_df_piece_day26,  st_unst_df_piece_day27,  st_unst_df_piece_day28,  st_unst_df_piece_day29,  st_unst_df_piece_day30,  st_unst_df_piece_day31,  st_unst_df_piece_day32,  st_unst_df_piece_day33,  st_unst_df_piece_day34,  st_unst_df_piece_day35,  st_unst_df_piece_day36,  st_unst_df_piece_day37,  st_unst_df_piece_day38,  st_unst_df_piece_day39,  st_unst_df_piece_day40,  st_unst_df_piece_day41,  st_unst_df_piece_day42,  st_unst_df_piece_day43,  st_unst_df_piece_day44)
```
